# Application Security API Documentation

## API Reference
- [List activation history](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-activation-history.md): __All products__ Lists the activation history for a configuration. The history is an array in descending order of `submitDate`. The most recent submitted activation lists first.
- [Get activation status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-activation.md): __All products__ Returns the status of an activation.
- [Get an activation request status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-activations-status.md): __All products__ Returns the status of a long-running activation request. Any errors that occur when generating the activation cause this API to respond with the underlying error status.
- [Activate a configuration version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-activations.md): __All products__ Activates one or more configurations globally. Note that you need to explicitly list any invalid hosts in the `acknowledgedInvalidHostsByConfig` parameter in order to successfully activate a configuration that includes invalid hostnames. If you have an invalid hostname that isn't explicitly called out you'll receive an error telling you to add them to `acknowledgedInvalidHostsByConfig`. See [Activate with invalid hostnames](ref:activate-with-invalid-hostnames) for instructions.
- [Compare two versions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-config-versions-diff.md): __All products__ This operation shows you differences between configuration versions. The `to` version is relative to the `from` version, and the versions you compare can be non-sequential. This means the `from` value can be more a more recent version than the `to` value. For more information, see [Diff configuration versions](ref:diff-configuration-versions).
- [Get asynchronous export results](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-export-config-version-async-result.md): __All products__ Get a security configuration version's export data from a previously completed asynchronous task. This is only available when the task status is `COMPLETED`.
- [Get asynchronous export status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-export-config-version-async-task-status.md): __All products__ Get a security configuration version's export status. When `PENDING`, the task is still running. When `COMPLETED`, the task finished successfully and you can collect the results through the link in the response. A status of `FAILED` appears when the task can't complete.
- [Export a configuration version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-export-config-version.md): __All products__ Returns comprehensive details about a security configuration version. This operation returns more data than [Get configuration version details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-version-number), including Bot Manager protections, rate and security policies, rules, hostnames, and numerous additional settings.
- [Asynchronously export a configuration version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-export-config-version-async-task.md): __All products__ Start an asynchronous export of a security configuration version. The response contains an `exportId` you can use to monitor progress and get the export data once it's ready.
- [API concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/api-concepts.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/404.md)
- [409](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/409.md)
- [429](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/429.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/api-errors.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/api-summary.md)
- [Activate with invalid hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/activate-with-invalid-hostnames.md)
- [Add a new custom rule to a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/add-a-new-custom-rule-to-a-configuration.md): These steps show you how to modify a configuration, add a new custom rule, and activate the new configuration version.
- [Diff configuration versions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/diff-configuration-versions.md)
- [Export a configuration version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/export-a-configuration-version.md)
- [API workflows](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/api-workflow.md)
- [Add a new hostname to a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/modify-and-activate-a-config-version.md): This workflow shows you how to make changes to a configuration version including adding new hostnames, and then activating the changes you've made.
- [Application Security API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/api.md)
- [Export match condition type values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/cndval.md)
- [CustomRule condition type values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/condition-type-values.md)
- [Condition values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/condition-values.md)
- [Deny name values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/deny-name-values.md)
- [Export condition type values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/etval.md)
- [Exception selector values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/exception-selector-values.md)
- [Export header values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/hdval.md)
- [Logging option values (Beta)](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/logging-option-values.md)
- [Recommendation selector values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/recommendation-selector-values.md)
- [SIEM action and attack-type exceptions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/siem-action-and-attack-type-exceptions.md)
- [Get Started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/getting-started.md)
- [Rate limiting](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/rate-limiting.md)
- [Get the attack payload log settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-advanced-settings-attack-payload-logging.md): __All products__ Lists attack payload log settings for a configuration. When set to `NONE`, the request body and response body show as redacted in the log. For example, `JSON_PAIRS:redacted`. Attack payload logging is on by default, and in most cases you should leave it enabled. This operation applies at the configuration level, and therefore applies to all policies within a configuration. If you want to view these settings for a specific policy, run [Get attack payload logging settings for a policy](ref:get-policies-attack-payload-logging).
- [Modify attack payload log settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-advanced-settings-attack-payload-logging.md): __All products__ Enable, disable, or update attack payload log settings for a configuration. When set to `NONE`, the request body and response body show as redacted in the log. For example, `JSON_PAIRS:redacted`. When set to `RESPONSE_BODY` or `REQUEST_BODY` the log shows only the string flagged as a possible attack, and not the entire request or response body. This operation applies at the configuration level, and therefore applies to all policies within a configuration. If you want to override these settings for a specific policy, run [Modify attack payload log settings for a policy](ref:put-policies-attack-payload-logging).
- [Remove a Behavioral DDoS profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-behavioral-ddos-profile.md): Deletes the specified Behavioral DDoS profile. You can't delete a Behavioral DDoS profile that's actively in use. To delete an active Behavioral DDoS profile, either activate an older configuration version or create a new version without the policy in place. _Products:_ All.
- [List Behavioral DDoS profiles](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-behavioral-ddos-profiles.md): Returns all Behavioral DDoS profiles for a specific security configuration version. _Products:_ All.
- [Create a Behavioral DDoS profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-behavioral-ddos-profile.md): Creates a new Behavioral DDoS profile for a specific configuration version. _Products:_ All.
- [Get a Behavioral DDoS profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-behavioral-ddos-profile.md): Returns the specified Behavioral DDoS profile. _Products:_ All.
- [Modify a Behavioral DDoS profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-behavioral-ddos-profile.md): Updates the specified Behavioral DDoS profile. _Products:_ All.
- [Get bypass network lists settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-bypass-network-lists.md): __Web Application Protector__ Lists which network lists are used in the bypass network lists settings. If you want to add or remove IP addresses from the network lists, use the [Network Lists API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/network-lists/reference/api).
- [Modify the bypass network lists settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-bypass-network-lists.md): __Web Application Protector__ Update which network lists to use in the bypass network lists settings. If you want to add or remove IP addresses from the network lists, use the [Network Lists API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/network-lists/reference/api).
- [Get cookie settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-advanced-settings-cookie-settings.md): __All products__ Returns cookie settings for a configuration. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Modify cookie settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-advanced-settings-cookie-settings.md): __All products__ Modify cookie settings for a configuration. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Get evasive path match settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-evasive-path-match-per-config.md): __Web Application Protector__, __Kona Site Defender__ Lists the evasive path match setting for a configuration. Evasive path match is off by default. This setting applies at the configuration level and applies to all policies within a configuration. If you want to view this setting for a specific policy, run [Get evasive patch match settings](ref:get-evasive-path-match).
- [Modify evasive path match settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-evasive-path-match-per-config.md): __Web Application Protector__, __Kona Site Defender__ Enable, disable, or update evasive path match settings for a configuration. This operation applies at the configuration level and applies to all policies within a configuration. If you want to override these settings for a specific policy, run [Modify evasive path match settings](ref:put-evasive-path-match).
- [List failover hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-failover-hostnames.md): __All products__ Get a list of the failover hostnames in a configuration.
- [Delete a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-config.md): __All products__ Deletes the specified security configuration.
- [Delete a configuration version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-version-number.md): __All products__ Delete the specified configuration version. You can't delete a version that's actively in use.
- [List configuration versions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-config-versions.md): __All products__ Lists available versions for the specified security configuration, with results optionally paginated.
- [Get a security configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-config.md): __All products__ Retrieves the configuration details.
- [List configurations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-configs.md): __All products__ Lists available security configurations.
- [Get the version notes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-version-notes.md): __All products__ Get the most recent version notes for a configuration.
- [Get configuration version details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-version-number.md): __All products__ Returns basic details about a configuration version. To get a more extensive object with detailed information about a version's security policies, rate policies, rules, and other additional settings, run the [Export a configuration version](ref:get-export-config-version) operation.
- [Clone a configuration version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-config-versions.md): __All products__ Creates a new version of the specified security configuration.
- [Create a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-config.md): __All products__ Create a new WAP or KSD security configuration. KSD security configurations start out empty, and WAP configurations are created with preset values. The contract you pass in the request body determines which product you use.  You can edit the default settings included in the WAP configuration, but you'll need to run additional operations in this API to select specific protections for KSD. Your KSD configuration needs match targets and protection settings before you activate.
- [Rename a security configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-config.md): __All products__ Update the name of your security configuration.
- [Modify version notes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-version-notes.md): __All products__ Update the most recent version notes for a configuration.
- [List hostname overlaps](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-hostname-coverage-overlapping.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ List the configuration versions that contain a hostname also included in the current configuration version.
- [List selectable hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-selectable-hostnames.md): __All products__ List the hostnames that a given configuration version has the ability to protect. Hostnames may show as error hosts when they aren't currently available. For example, when a contract expires.
- [List selected hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-selected-hostnames.md): __All products__ List the hostnames that the configuration version selects as candidates of protected hostnames, which you can use in match targets.
- [Modify selected hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-selected-hostnames.md): __All products__ Update the list of selected hostnames for a configuration version. Now you can add an individual hostname to your selected hostnames list without passing the entire list plus the new hostname in this PUT object. Previously, you needed to pass every hostname in the PUT object or any hostnames not included would be deleted from the list.
- [Get the HTTP header log settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-advanced-settings-logging.md): __All products__ Lists HTTP header logging controls for a configuration. HTTP header logging is on by default, and in most cases you should leave it enabled. You can filter requests by header type, including or excluding requests with a specific header, or by cookie. This operation applies at the configuration level, and therefore applies to all policies within a configuration. If you want to view these settings for a specific policy, run [Get HTTP header log settings](ref:get-policies-logging).
- [Modify HTTP header log settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-advanced-settings-logging.md): __All products__ Enable, disable, or update HTTP header Logging settings for a configuration. This operation applies at the configuration level, and therefore applies to all policies within a configuration. If you want to override these settings for a specific policy, run [Modify HTTP header log settings](ref:put-policies-logging).
- [Get JA4 client TLS fingerprint settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-ja4-fingerprint-settings.md): __Beta__, __Web Application Protector__, __Kona Site Defender__ Return JA4 client TLS fingerprint settings for a configuration. JA4 is a client-focused fingerprint based on the client TLS handshake. Use JA4 fingerprinting to mitigate DDoS attacks, detect and mitigate bots, and block known attackers from sending application layer attacks payloads. [Learn more about JA4 fingerprinting]( https://techdocs.akamai.com/app-api-protector/docs/adv-settings).
- [Modify JA4 client TLS fingerprint settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-ja4-fingerprint-settings.md): __Beta__, __Web Application Protector__, __Kona Site Defender__ Update JA4 client TLS fingerprint settings for a configuration.
- [Remove a match target](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-match-target.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Deletes the specified match target.
- [Get the hostname coverage match targets](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-coverage-match-targets.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ List the API and website match targets that protect a hostname.
- [Get a match target](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-match-target.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Returns the specified match target.
- [List match targets](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-match-targets.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Returns match targets defined in the specified security configuration version.
- [Create a match target](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-match-targets.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Creates a new Match Target in the specified Configuration Version.
- [Modify a match target](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-match-target.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Updates details about the specified match target.
- [Modify match target order](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-match-targets-sequence.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Updates the sequence of Match Targets in a configuration version. The `website` and `api` match targets' sequence requires updates from separate requests by passing the `type` attribute in the JSON request.
- [Get PII learning settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-advanced-settings-pii-learning.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Shows whether PII learning is enabled. This operation applies at the security configuration level.
- [Enable PII learning settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-advanced-settings-pii-learning.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Turn on PII learning for your API definition. To enable PII learning, submit your request with a payload of `{"enablePiiLearning": true}`. To disable PII learning, submit your request with a payload of `{"enablePiiLearning": false}`. This operation applies at the security configuration level. Run [Modify PII settings for an endpoint version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/api-definitions/reference/post-endpoint-version-pii-settings) to manage your API definitions in regards to PII.
- [Get Pragma settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-advanced-settings-pragma-header.md): __All products__ Return the `Pragma` header excluded conditions. By default, the `Pragma` header is stripped from an operation's response except in cases where you set `excludeCondition`. This operation applies at the security configuration level. To see settings at the security policy level, run [List Pragma header settings for a security policy](ref:get-policies-pragma-header).
- [Modify Pragma settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-advanced-settings-pragma-header.md): __All products__ Update the `Pragma` header excluded conditions. By default, the `Pragma` header is stripped from an operation's response except in cases where you set `excludeCondition`. To remove existing settings, submit your request with an empty payload `{}` at the top-level of an object. For example, submit `"type": "{}"` in the request body to remove the `requestHeaderValueMatch` from the excluded conditions. If you submit an empty payload for each field, you'll clear all of your condition settings. This operation applies at the security configuration level. To see settings at the security policy level, run [Modify Pragma header settings for a security policy](ref:put-policies-pragma-header).
- [Get prefetch requests](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-advanced-settings-prefetch.md): __Kona Site Defender__ Get Prefetch Request settings.
- [Modify prefetch requests](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-advanced-settings-prefetch.md): __Kona Site Defender__ Enabling this protection causes your application firewall rules to inspect internal requests (those between your origin and Akamai's servers) for file types you specify. You can also apply rate controls to prefetch requests. This operation applies at the configuration level.
- [Get request body size settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-advanced-settings-request-body.md): __All products__ Return request body inspection limit settings for a configuration. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Modify request body inspection limit settings for a configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-advanced-settings-request-body.md): __All products__ Modify request body inspection limit settings for a configuration. Inspect request bodies up to a certain size. In exceptional cases, you can change the default value to a set limit: `8`, `16`, or `32` kb. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Remove a custom deny action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-custom-deny.md): __All products__ Delete a custom deny action. You can't delete a custom deny action that is actively in use. To delete the custom deny action, first activate an older configuration version or create a new version without the policy in place.
- [Get a custom deny action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-custom-deny-action.md): __All products__ Returns the specified custom deny action.
- [List custom deny actions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-custom-deny-actions.md): __All products__ Returns custom deny actions for a specific security configuration version.
- [Create a custom deny action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-custom-deny.md): __All products__ Create a new custom deny action for a specific configuration version.
- [Modify a custom deny action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-custom-deny.md): __All products__ Update details for a specific custom deny action.
- [Remove a custom rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-config-custom-rule.md): __All products__ Deletes a custom rule as long as it isn't activated.
- [Get a custom rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-config-custom-rule.md): __All products__ Returns the details of a custom rule.
- [List custom rules](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-configs-custom-rules.md): __All products__ Lists custom rules defined in a security configuration.
- [List custom rules usage by security policies](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-config-custom-rules-usage.md): __All products__ Returns a list of security policies using each requested custom rule with a set action (Allow or Deny), if any apply. Skips custom rule IDs that do not belong to this security configuration.
- [Create a custom rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-config-custom-rules.md): __All products__ Creates a new custom rule.
- [Modify a custom rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-config-custom-rule.md): __All products__ Updates an existing custom rule.
- [Remove a malware policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-malware-policy.md): __All products__ Delete a specific malware policy that's not actively in use. To delete an active malware policy, first activate an older configuration version or create a new version without the policy in place.
- [List malware policies](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-malware-policies.md): __All products__ Return malware policies for a security configuration version.
- [List supported malware policy content types](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-malware-policy-content-types.md): __All products__ Returns all the supported malware policy content types for a security configuration version.
- [Get a malware policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-malware-policy.md): __All products__ Return a malware policy.
- [Create a malware policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-malware-policies.md): __All products__ Create a new malware policy for a security configuration version.
- [Modify a malware policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-malware-policy.md): __All products__ Update details for a malware policy.
- [Remove a rate policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-rate-policy.md): __All products__ Delete the specified rate policy. You can't delete a rate policy that is actively in use. To delete the rate policy, first activate an older configuration version or create a new version without the policy in place.
- [List rate policies](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-rate-policies.md): __All products__ Returns rate policies for a specific security configuration version.
- [Get a rate policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-rate-policy.md): __All products__ Returns the specified rate policy.
- [Create a rate policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-rate-policies.md): __All products__ Create a new rate policy for a specific configuration version. Now you can match on defined or undefined resources. If you're setting a match for either resource type, both `definedResources` and `undefinedResources` must be present in the request object or the request is considered incomplete. When `true`, match on _any_ defined resources without passing a `resourceId`. When `false`, you'll need to pass a `resourceId`. If you pass `definedResources` and `undefinedResources` with empty values, they default to `false`. You can omit both resources and use this operation without these new match criteria. Contact your account team if you'd like to match on `definedResources` or `undefinedResources`.
- [Modify a rate policy evaluation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-rate-policy-evaluation.md): __All products__ Apply or discard a rate policy evaluation.
- [Modify a rate policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-rate-policy.md): __All products__ Update details for a specific rate policy. Now you can match on defined or undefined resources. If you're setting a match for either resource type, both `definedResources` and `undefinedResources` must be present in the request object or the request is considered incomplete. When `true`, match on _any_ defined resources without passing a `resourceId`. When `false`, you'll need to pass a `resourceId`. If you pass `definedResources` and `undefinedResources` with empty values, they default to `false`. You can omit both resources and use this operation without these new match criteria. Contact your account team if you'd like to match on `definedResources` or `undefinedResources`.
- [Remove a reputation profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-reputation-profile.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Delete a reputation profile. You can't delete a reputation profile that is actively in use. To delete the reputation profile, first activate an older configuration version or create a new version without that policy in place.
- [Get a reputation profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-reputation-profile.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Returns the details for a specific reputation profile.
- [List reputation profiles](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-reputation-profiles.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Returns reputation profiles for a specific security configuration version. To use reputation profiles, you need to add [Client Reputation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/app-api-protector/docs/control-client-reputation) to Kona Site Defender on your contract.
- [Create a reputation profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-reputation-profiles.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Create a new reputation profile for a specific configuration version.

- [List evaluation hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-selected-hostnames-eval-hostnames.md): __Web Application Protector__, __App & API Protector__ List the evaluation hostnames for a configuration version. Evaluation mode for hostnames is only available for Web Application Protector and App & API Protector. Run hostnames in evaluation mode to see how your configuration settings protect traffic for that hostname before adding a hostname directly to a live configuration. Once you begin, the hostnames you evaluate start responding to traffic as if they are your current hostnames. However, instead of taking an action the evaluation hostnames log which action they would have taken if they were your actively protected hostnames and not a test.
- [Protect evaluation hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-protect-eval-hostnames.md): __Web Application Protector__, __App & API Protector__ Move hostnames you're evaluating to active protection. When you move a hostname from the evaluation hostnames list, it's added to your security policy as a protected hostname. You'll see that hostname in the response object the next time you run [List selected hostnames](ref:get-selected-hostnames).
- [Modify evaluation hostnames](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-selected-eval-hostnames.md): __Web Application Protector__, __App & API Protector__ Update the list of hostnames you want to evaluate for a configuration version.
- [Get the conditions and exceptions for an evaluation rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-condition-exception.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ List the conditions and exceptions for a rule you want to evaluate. This operation returns the same object type as other condition and exception operations. Keep in mind that the response for this operation applies to the rules you're evaluating even though the concept of conditions and exceptions is the same for all condition exception endpoints.
- [Get the exceptions of an evaluation attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-eval-group-condition-exception.md): __All products__ List an attack group's exceptions.
- [Modify the conditions and exceptions for an evaluation rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-condition-exception.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Update an evaluation rule's conditions and exceptions. When the conditions are met, the rule's actions are ignored and not applied to that specific traffic. The rule action you update with this operation is for a rule you are evaluating and not a rule currently in use for your site or app.
- [Modify the exceptions of an evaluation attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-eval-group-condition-exception.md): __All products__ Update an evaluation attack group exceptions.
- [Get the action for an evaluation attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-eval-group.md): __All products__ Currently the only field in the response object is `action`, which displays the action for the evaluation attack group.
- [List evaluation attack groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-eval-groups.md): __All products__ Return a list of evaluation attack groups with their associated actions.
- [Modify the action for an evaluation attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-eval-group.md): __All products__ Update what action to take when an evaluation attack group's rule triggers. Use `alert` to record the trigger of the event, `deny` to block the request, or `none` to take no action. Currently the only field in the attack group object is `action`.
- [List evaluation hostnames for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-eval-hostnames.md): __Web Application Protector__, __App & API Protector__ List the evaluation hostnames for a configuration version. - [Protect evaluation hostnames for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-protect-eval-hostnames-per-policy.md): __Web Application Protector__, __App & API Protector__ Move hostnames you're evaluating to active protection. - [Set evaluation mode](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-policy-eval.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Evaluation mode runs concurrently with your existing Web Application Firewall Rule settings and records how the rules would respond if they were applied to live traffic. The default action for evaluation rules is `alert`. Unlike other POST or PUT actions, this request object supports values other than `enabled` or `disabled`. Use `START` to begin evaluation mode. An evaluation period lasts four weeks unless you stop the evaluation. Once you begin, the rules you evaluate will respond to traffic as if they are your current rules. However, instead of taking an action the evaluation rules will log which action they would have taken if they were your active rules and not a test of future rules. Use `STOP` to end the evaluation before it completes on its own, and not upgrade your rules. Use `RESTART` to start an evaluation you previously stopped, or one that has expired. Use `COMPLETE` to stop your in-progress evaluation and automatically upgrade your existing KRS rules to the rule set you just evaluated. Use `UPDATE` to upgrade to the newest available version of evaluation rules. A request value of `START`, `RESTART`, or `COMPLETE` returns an `eval` value of `enabled`. A request value of `STOP` or `UPDATE` returns an `eval` value of `disabled`.   If you're using Adaptive Security Engine, pass `eval` as usual, and add `"mode": "ASE_AUTO"` or `ASE_MANUAL` in your request. If you're using Kona Rule Sets, continue to pass only the `eval` value.
- [Get the penalty box for a policy in evaluation mode](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-eval-policy-penalty-box.md): __All products__ Returns the penalty box settings for a security policy in evaluation mode. When the penalty box is enabled for a policy, clients that trigger a Web Application Firewall deny action are placed in the penalty box. There, the action you select for penalty box (either `alert` or `deny`) continues to apply to any requests from that client for the next 10 minutes.
- [Modify the evaluation penalty box](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-eval-policy-penalty-box.md): __All products__ Update the penalty box settings for a security policy in evaluation mode. If set to `true`, you can choose to `deny` requests coming from a client in the penalty box, or trigger an `alert` instead.
- [Get the action of an evaluation rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-eval-rule.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Return the action for a specific rule you want to evaluate. This applies to both ASE auto and ASE manual upgrade modes. You may need additional permissions to add or update rule actions in `ASE_AUTO` mode.
- [List evaluation rules](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-eval-rules.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Return the rules available for evaluation and their actions.
- [Modify the action of an evaluation rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-eval-rule.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Update the action for a specific rule you want to evaluate. Like your current rules, actions are `alert` to record the trigger of the event, `deny` to block the request, or `none` to take no action. This applies to both ASE auto and ASE manual upgrade modes. You may need additional permissions to add or update rule actions in `ASE_AUTO` mode.
- [Get penalty box conditions in evaluation mode](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-eval-policy-penalty-box-conditions.md): __All products__ Returns the penalty box condition settings for a security policy in evaluation mode.
- [Modify the penalty box conditions in evaluation mode](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-eval-policy-penalty-box-conditions.md): __All products__ Modifies the penalty box condition settings for a security policy in evaluation mode.

This operation only lists the endpoints. To manage them, use [Register an endpoint](https://techdocs.akamai.com/api-gateway/reference/post-endpoints) from the API Endpoint Definition API.
- [List API request constraints and actions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-api-request-constraints.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Return a list of APIs with their constraints and associated actions.
- [Modify an API request constraint's action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-api-request-constraints-api.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Update what action to take when the API request constraint triggers. This operation modifies an individual API constraint action. To use this operation, run [List all API request constraints and actions](ref:get-api-request-constraints) and pick an API from the response list. Use `alert` to record the trigger of the event, `deny` to block the request, or `none` to take no action. Currently the only field in the API Request Constraints object is `action`.
- [Modify the request constraint action for all APIs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-api-request-constraints.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Update what action to take when any API request constraint triggers. Use `alert` to record the trigger of the event, `deny` to block the request, or `customDenyId` to apply a custom deny response.
- [Get attack payload logging settings for a policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policies-attack-payload-logging.md): __All products__ List a security policy's settings for attack payload logging. Attack payload logging is on by default, and in most cases you should leave it enabled. When set to `NONE`, the request body and response body appear as `redacted` in the log. For example, `JSON_PAIRS:redacted`. This operation applies at the security policy level, and overrides the attack payload logging settings at the configuration level on a per-policy basis.
- [Modify attack payload logging settings for a policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policies-attack-payload-logging.md): __All products__ Update attack payload logging settings for a specific policy. This operation applies at the security policy level, and overrides the attack payload logging settings at the configuration level on a per-policy basis. When set to `NONE`, the request body and response body appear as `redacted` in the log. For example, `JSON_PAIRS:redacted`.

Use this action type to insert a challenge, like a CAPTCHA, into the user workflow. You can also apply your own branding to the action to match your website. - [Remove a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-policy.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Delete the specified security policy. You can't delete a security policy that is actively in use. Before you run this operation, create a new version of your security configuration and omit the policy you want to delete. Once you have activated your new configuration version, you can delete the security policy you omitted from the new version. One way to create a new configuration quickly is to run [Clone a configuration version](ref:post-config-versions), remove the policy you want to delete, then PUT the edited object back to [Modify a security policy](ref:put-policy).
- [List security policies](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policies.md): __All products__ Returns a list of security policies available for the specified security configuration.

- [Clone or create a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-policy.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Creates a new copy of an existing security policy or creates a new security policy from scratch when you don't specify a policy to clone in the request. To create a new security policy, leave `createFromSecurityPolicy` empty in your request.
- [Modify a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy.md): __All products__ Update the name of a specific security policy. Currently, you can only edit a security policy's name with this operation, although other editable fields will be available in the future. For any changes to settings within a security policy, run the modify operation for that specific setting. For example, to update your IP/Geo settings, run [Update IP/Geo Firewall settings](ref:put-policy-ip-geo-firewall).
- [List selected hostnames for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-selected-hostnames-per-policy.md): __Web Application Protector__, __App & API Protector__ List the hostnames that the configuration version selects as candidates of protected hostnames, which you can use in match targets.
- [Modify selected hostnames for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-selected-hostnames-per-config.md): __Web Application Protector__, __App & API Protector__ Update the list of selected hostnames for a configuration. You can add an individual hostname to your selected hostnames list without passing the entire list plus the new hostname in this PUT object. Previously, you needed to pass every hostname in the PUT object or any hostnames not included would be deleted from the list.
- [Get HTTP header log settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policies-logging.md): __All products__ List a security policy's settings for HTTP header logging. HTTP header logging is on by default, and in most cases you should leave it enabled. You can filter requests by header type, including or excluding requests with a specific header, or by cookie. This operation applies at the security policy level, and overrides the HTTP header log settings at the configuration level on a per-policy basis.
- [Modify HTTP header log settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policies-logging.md): __All products__ Enable, disable, or update HTTP header logging settings for a specific policy. This operation applies at the security policy level, and overrides the HTTP header log settings at the configuration level on a per-policy basis.
- [Get IP/Geo Firewall settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-ip-geo-firewall.md): __All products__ Lists which network lists are used in the IP/Geo Firewall settings. In Control Center this method is called `mode`. In this API the method is called `blocked`. The response shows blocked and allowed IPs depending on which network lists you include and which version of the mode you choose. If you want to add or remove IP addresses from the network lists, use the [Network Lists API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/network-lists/reference/api). Note that subnet controls are a legacy item in Control Center and are not available through this API.
- [Modify IP/Geo Firewall settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-ip-geo-firewall.md): __All products__ Update the method and which network lists to use for IP/Geo firewall blocking. In Control Center this method is called `mode`. In this API the method is called `blocked`. Use `blockSpecificIPGeo` to block any IPs, geographies, or network lists you choose with this setting. Use `blockAllTrafficExceptAllowedIPs` to allow specific IPs or geographies that you choose to let through while the rest remain blocked. IPs you want to allow are contained in the `allowedIPNetworkLists`. It's important to verify the IPs you block are the ones you intend to block as it's easy to block wanted traffic by accident. Note that subnet controls are a legacy item in Control Center and are not available through this API.
- [List malware policy actions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-malware-policies-actions.md): __All products__ Return a list of all malware policies currently in use with the actions each policy takes when conditions are met.
- [Modify a malware policy action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-malware-policy-action.md): __All products__ Update the actions in a malware policy.
- [Get Pragma settings for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policies-pragma-header.md): __All products__ Return the `Pragma` header's excluded conditions. By default, the `Pragma` header debugging information is stripped from an operation's response except in cases where you set `excludeCondition`. This operation applies at the security policy level. To modify `Pragma` header settings at the security configuration level, run [List Pragma header settings for a configuration](ref:get-advanced-settings-pragma-header).
- [Modify Pragma settings for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policies-pragma-header.md): __All products__ Update the pragma header excluded conditions. By default, the `Pragma` header debugging information is stripped from an operation's response except in cases where you set `excludeCondition`. To remove existing settings, submit your request with an empty payload `{}` at the top-level of an object. For example, submit `"type": "{}"` in the request body to remove the `requestHeaderValueMatch` from the excluded conditions. If you submit an empty payload for each field, you'll clear all of your condition settings. To modify `Pragma` header settings at the security configuration level, run [Modify Pragma header settings for a configuration](ref:put-advanced-settings-pragma-header) .
- [Get protections](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-protections.md): __All products__ Get the protections and whether they are enabled or disabled in a security policy.
- [Modify protections](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-protections.md): __All products__ Update the security policy protections. This applies a set of protections that you can enable individually.
- [List rate policy actions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-rate-policies-actions.md): __All products__ Returns a list of all rate policies currently in use with the actions each policy takes when conditions are met.
- [Modify a rate policy action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-rate-policy-action.md): __All products__ Updates the actions in a rate policy.
- [Get reputation analysis settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-reputation-analysis.md): __Kona Site Defender__ Return the current reputation analysis settings.
- [Modify reputation analysis settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-reputation-analysis.md): __Kona Site Defender__ Toggle the `forwardToHTTPHeader` and `forwardSharedIPToHTTPHeaderAndSIEM` options. The `forwardToHTTPHeader` option adds client reputation details to requests forwarded to origin in an HTTP header. The `forwardSharedIPToHTTPHeaderAndSIEM` option adds a value indicating that shared IPs are included in HTTP header and SIEM integration when used.
- [Get request body inspection limit settings for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policies-request-body.md): __All products__ Return request body inspection limit settings for a security policy. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Modify request body size settings for a security policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policies-request-body.md): __All products__ Update inspection settings for a security policy. Inspect request bodies up to a certain size. In exceptional cases, you can change the default value to a set limit: `8`, `16`, or `32` KB. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Get slow POST protection settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-slow-post.md): __All products__ Get slow POST protection settings for a specific policy.
- [Modify slow POST protection settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-slow-post.md): __All products__ Update slow POST protection settings for a specific policy.
- [List URL protection policy actions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-url-protection-policies-actions.md): __All products__ Returns a list of all URL protection policies currently in use, along with the actions each policy takes when conditions are met.
- [Modify a URL protection policy action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-url-protection-policy-action.md): __All products__ Updates the actions in a URL protection policy.
- [Get WAF Penalty Box settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-ase-penalty-box-settings.md): __Web Application Protector__, __Kona Site Defender__ Return WAF Penalty Box settings for a specific configuration.
- [Modify WAF Penalty Box settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-ase-penalty-box-settings.md): __Web Application Protector__, __Kona Site Defender__ Update WAF Penalty Box settings for a specific configuration.
- [Get the exceptions of an attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-attack-group-condition-exception.md): __All products__ List an attack group's exceptions.
- [Get the action for an attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-attack-group.md): __All products__ Currently the only field in the response object is `action`, which displays the action for the attack group.
- [List attack groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-attack-groups.md): __All products__ Return a list of attack groups with their associated actions.
- [Modify the exceptions of an attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-attack-group-condition-exception.md): __All products__ Update an attack group exceptions.
- [Modify the action for an attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-attack-group.md): __All products__ Update what action to take when an attack group's rule triggers. Use `alert` to record the trigger of the event, `deny` to block the request, or `none` to take no action. Currently the only field in the attack group object is `action`.
- [List rules](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-rules.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Returns the action taken for each rule in a policy. The action occurs when the rules are triggered by a request. These are not the same rules as Custom Rules that you configure yourself. These rules are maintained by Akamai.
- [Get the conditions and exceptions of a rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-rule-condition-exception.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ List a KRS rule's conditions and exceptions.
- [Get the action for a rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-rule.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Return the action a rule takes when triggered. This applies to both ASE auto and ASE manual upgrade modes. Rule actions for `ASE_AUTO` are currently in beta. Contact your account team to access this option.
- [Get adaptive intelligence settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-rules-threat-intel.md): __All products__ Returns whether adaptive intelligence is enabled. Adaptive intelligence allows our network to better tune rules for better accuracy. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Get upgrade details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-rules-upgrade-details.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Only applies to Kona rule sets. The KRS rule sets are maintained by Akamai's security research team. Run this operation before you upgrade to verify changes in the rule sets. If you want to test how the updates affect your site, you can run the updated rules in evaluation mode.
- [Get a security policy's rule set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-waf-policy-ruleset-composite.md): __All products__ Returns the security policy rule set, which includes the version, threat intelligence settings, rules, and attack group details.
- [Modify a security policy's rule set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/patch-waf-policy-ruleset-composite.md): __All products__ Modify the security policy's rule set including its rule and attack group details.
- [Upgrade KRS ruleset](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-rules.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__, __Adaptive Security Engine__ Upgrade to the most recent version of the KRS rule set. Akamai periodically updates these rules to keep protections current. However, the rules you use in your security policies do not automatically upgrade to the latest version when setting `mode` to `KRS`. These rules do update automatically when you have `mode` set to `AAG`. Before you upgrade, run [Get upgrade details](ref:get-rules-upgrade-details) to see which rules have changed. If you want to test how these rules would operate with live traffic before committing to the upgrade, run them in evaluation mode. This applies to KRS rules only and does not allow you to make any changes to the rules themselves. The response is the same as the `mode` response. If you're already using or want to switch from KRS to Adaptive Security Engine (ASE), pass `"upgrade": true,` and `"mode": "ASE_AUTO"` or `ASE_MANUAL` in your request. If you're using Kona Rule Sets, continue to pass only the upgrade value. Note that this operation does not grant access to Adaptive Security Engine. It updates the ASE protections if you already have ASE on your contract. Once you switch to ASE protections, you can't go back to KRS rules. Contact your account team if you want to add ASE to your contract.
- [Modify the conditions and exceptions of a rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-rule-condition-exception.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Update a rule's conditions and exceptions. When the conditions are met, the rule's actions are ignored and _not_ applied to that specific traffic.
- [Modify the action for a rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-rule.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Update what action a rule takes when it's triggered. This applies to both ASE auto and ASE manual upgrade modes. Rule actions for `ASE_AUTO` are currently in beta. Contact your account team to access this option. Use `alert` to record the trigger of the event, `deny` to block the request, or `none` to take no action.
- [Modify adaptive intelligence settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-rules-threat-intel.md): __All products__ Only applies to Adaptive Security Engine (ASE) manual rule sets. Enable or disable adaptive intelligence. Contact your account team if you'd like to run this operation.
- [Get penalty box condition](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-penalty-box-conditions.md): __All products__ Returns the penalty box condition settings for a security policy.
- [Modify the penalty box conditions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-penalty-box-conditions.md): __All products__ Modifies the penalty box conditions settings for a security policy.
- [Get the penalty box](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-penalty-box.md): __All products__ Returns the penalty box settings for the security policy you specify.
- [Modify the penalty box](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-penalty-box.md): __All products__ Modifies the penalty box settings for a security policy. When the penalty box is enabled for a policy, clients that trigger a WAF Deny action are placed in the _penalty box_. There, the action you select for penalty box (either `Alert` or `Deny`) continues to apply to any requests from that client for the next 10 minutes.
- [Get a rapid rule's action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-rapid-rule-action.md): __All products__ Returns the action for a single rapid rule. Run [Update a rapid rule's action](doc:put-policy-rapid-rule-action) if you want to set different actions for each rule, or override the default action for a single rule.
- [List a rapid rule's conditions and exceptions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-rapid-rule-condition-exception.md): __All products__ Returns the conditions and exceptions set for the rapid rule.
- [Get a rapid rule's lock status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-rapid-rule-lock.md): __All products__ Returns the rapid rule lock status. When locked, the action set for the rapid rule applies to all new versions of that rule.
- [Get rapid rules' default action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-rapid-rules-action.md): __All products__ Returns the default action set for all rapid rules. To override this setting for an individual rule, run [Update a rapid rule's action](doc:put-policy-rapid-rule-action).
- [Get rapid rules' status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-rapid-rules-status.md): __All products__ Returns whether rapid rules are enabled or disabled.
- [List rapid rules](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-rapid-rules.md): __All products__ Returns rapid rules. [Rapid rules](https://techdocs.akamai.com/app-api-protector/docs/rapid-rules) are WAF rules we are actively testing and refining. You can start using them while we revise them. Once we complete fine-tuning a rule, it becomes a WAF rule you can access through [List rules](doc:get-policy-rules).
- [Update a rapid rule's action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-rapid-rule-action.md): __All products__ Set the rapid rule's action. Run this operation if you want to set different actions for each rule, or override the default action for a single rule.
- [Update a rapid rule's conditions and exceptions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-rapid-rule-condition-exception.md): __All products__ Set the rapid rule's conditions and exceptions.
- [Update a rapid rule's lock status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-rapid-rule-lock.md): __All products__ Set the rapid rule lock status. When enabled, the action set for the rapid rule applies to all new versions of that rule.
- [Update rapid rules' default action](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-rapid-rules-action.md): __All products__ Set rapid rules default action. This applies to _all_ rapid rules by default unless you specifically override this setting for an individual rule with [Update a rapid rule's action](doc:put-policy-rapid-rule-action).
- [Update rapid rules' status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-rapid-rules-status.md): __All products__ Enable or disable all rapid rules.
- [List tuning recommendations for an attack group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-attack-group.md): __Adaptive Security Engine__ Returns available tuning recommendations for an attack group. Our system can identify patterns of false positives, and suggests exceptions for you to include in your attack group.
- [List tuning recommendations for a rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-recommendations-rule.md): __Adaptive Security Engine__ Returns available tuning recommendations for a rule. Our system can identify patterns of false positives, and suggests exceptions for you to include in your rule. In cases where your rules are already optimum, the response payload returns empty.
- [Get tuning recommendations for a policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-recommendations.md): __Adaptive Security Engine__ Lists available tuning recommendations for a policy. Our system can identify patterns of false positives, and suggests exceptions for you to include in your policy.
- [Respond to exception recommendations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-recommendations.md): __Adaptive Security Engine__ Accept, decline, or reset the recommended exception. Accepting a tuning recommendation creates or updates the exception for the attack group. Use `reset` to restore a declined recommendation to a neutral state so you can accept it or decline it again later.
- [Get the current mode](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-policy-mode.md): __All products__ When using Web Application Firewall in your security policy, this mode conveys how you're planning to keep the rules up to date, either `KRS` for manual updates or `AAG` for automatic updates. This operation returns which mode your rules are currently set to.
- [Modify the mode](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-policy-mode.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__, __Adaptive Security Engine__ The mode you set determines how your rule sets are updated. Use `KRS` mode to update the rule sets manually, or `AAG` to have them update automatically. If you're using Adaptive Security Engine, use `ASE_AUTO` to update the rules automatically, or `ASE_MANUAL` to update them yourself.
- [Get an onboarding activation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboarding-activation-status.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Returns the status of an activation. Activations can take about 20 minutes. If the activation fails, to specific information about the failure is returned in the error response.
- [Activate an onboarding](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-onboarding-activation.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Activate an onboarding on the staging or production networks. You need to call the Domain Validation APIs to confirm domain ownership before proceeding. You need to activate on the staging network before you can activate on production. Note that with this operation you can only activate on one network at a time. Once you activate on staging, you can test your site as you normally would when using Property Manager or any of our security products.
- [Delete an onboarding](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/delete-onboarding.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Delete a specific onboarding. You can't delete an onboarding that has an activation in progress. To delete an onboarding that's activating, wait for the activation to complete, then run this operation.
- [Get onboarding settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboarding-settings.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Gets the settings for a specific onboarding.
- [Get an onboarding](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboarding.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Get an onboarding's details. Once you've completed all steps in the onboarding process, run this call again to verify `onboardingStatus: "COMPLETE"`.
- [List onboardings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboardings.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Lists onboardings. You can filter results by hostname or onboarding status.
- [Create an onboarding](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-onboarding.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Create a new onboarding. To start, you'll need a valid set of hostnames that either need delivery settings, an Akamai-owned certificate from the Certificate Provisioning System (CPS), and security protections, or have delivery settings complete and only need security protections. However, you can include only one type of these hostnames per onboarding, not both. If you have both kinds of hostnames, those that need delivery settings, a CPS certificate, and security protections, and those that only need security protections, you'll need to create two onboarding objects. The response object is your newly created onboarding.
- [Modify onboarding settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-onboarding-settings.md): __App & API Protector__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Update the settings in an onboarding. You can't update settings for an onboarding object that has already activated on staging or production. You can, however, change settings for an onboarding that has not been activated.
- [List onboarding certificate challenges](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboarding-certificate-validation.md): __All products__ Returns the information you need to validate your certificate. Add the `TXT` DNS record listed in the  response to your DNS record. Adding the `TXT` record won't affect your website. Once you've added the `TXT` record, and before the token expires, run [Validate onboarding certificate](doc:post-onboarding-certificate-validation) to validate the certificate.
- [List hostname CNAME DNS records](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboarding-cname-records.md): __All products__ Returns the DNS records you need to CNAME to Akamai. Once you update your CNAME record with the information in this call's response, your end user traffic starts to flow through Akamai's platform. Note that you can only CNAME to Akamai after an activation on staging or production is complete.
- [List onboarding domain challenges](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboarding-domain-validation.md): __All products__ Returns the information you need to validate your domains. Add the `TXT` or `CNAME` value listed in the response to your DNS record. Adding the record won't affect your website. Once you've added the record, and before the token expires, run [Validate onboarding domains](doc:post-onboarding-domain-validation).
- [List origin hostname DNS records](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-onboarding-origin-validation.md): __All products__ Lists the DNS records for your origin. Once you make this call, visit your DNS provider account and create a new DNS type `A` record with the values returned in the response. In the rare case that you changed your origin values outside this setup, you can run [Skip origin validation](doc:post-skip-onboarding-origin-validation) to proceed to the next step without validating your origin at this time.
- [Validate onboarding certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-onboarding-certificate-validation.md): __All products__ Validates the certificate's `TXT` DNS record. Once validation completes, it takes about five minutes to deploy the certificates across Akamai's global network. You can validate with HTTP token, DNS records, or URL redirect.
- [Validate onboarding domains](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-onboarding-domain-validation.md): __All products__ Verifies the domain validation challenges. Onboarding activations can't proceed unless your onboarding hostnames are domain-validated using this API call. You can validate with HTTP token, DNS records, or URL redirect.
- [Validate origin hostnames DNS records](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-onboarding-origin-validation.md): __All products__ Validates the `A` DNS record you entered into your DNS record. In the rare case that you changed your origin values outside this setup, you can run [Skip origin validation](doc:post-skip-onboarding-origin-validation) to proceed to the next step without validating your origin at this time.
- [Skip origin hostnames DNS record validation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-skip-onboarding-origin-validation.md): __All products__ Skips the validation of `A` DNS record. In the rare case that you changed your origin values outside this onboarding, you can choose not to complete the origin validation process through this onboarding workflow. Skipping for any other reason may cause your site to become unreachable. When you choose to skip validating your origin through this call, the response shows the records with a value of `skipped`.
- [Validate hostname CNAME DNS records](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-validate-onboarding-cname-records.md): __All products__ Validates your site is ready to serve traffic. Call this operation after you've replaced the DNS entry that directs traffic to your site with the DNS CNAME records in [List hostname CNAME DNS records](doc:get-onboarding-cname-records). In each record, set the time-to-live (TTL) to 300 seconds (5 minutes). Once this call completes, run [Get an onboarding](doc:get-onboarding) to verify your onboarding is complete. When you've successfully completed any validations and CNAME changes, you'll see `onboardingStatus: "COMPLETE"` in the response.
- [List available hostnames for a new configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-selectable-hostnames-per-config.md): __All products__ Lists the hostnames for a given contract and group. Use this operation for a new configuration, and use [List selectable hostnames](ref:get-selectable-hostnames) to see a list of hostnames you can add to an existing configuration. This operation shows you every acceptable hostname you can use, where the other hostname operation omits any hostnames already included in your configuration.
- [List contracts and groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-contracts-groups.md): __All products__ List the contracts and groups for your account. Each object contains the contract, groups associated with the contract, and whether Kona Site Defender or Web Application Protector is the product for that contract. You'll need this information when you create a new security configuration or when you want to get a list of hostnames still available for use in a security policy.
- [Get a CVE](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-cve.md): Get details for a specific CVE.
- [List CVEs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-cves.md): List CVEs currently in Akamai's CVE catalog. The catalog contains only CVEs the Akamai Threat Research team is aware of. App & API Protector can identify and block attacks related to the active exploitation of many more CVEs and attacks without associated known vulnerabilities.
- [Get CVE coverage](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-security-coverage.md): The coverages provided by your configurations, attack groups, and rules.
- [List subscribed CVEs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-subscribed.md): List the CVEs you are subscribed to. To add or remove CVEs, run [Subscribe to CVEs](doc:post-subscribe) or [Unsubscribe from CVEs](doc:post-unsubscribe) accordingly.
- [Subscribe to CVEs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-subscribe.md): Subscribe to notifications for specific CVEs.
- [Unsubscribe from CVEs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-unsubscribe.md): Unsubscribe from notifications about all CVEs, or specific CVEs.
- [Get a discovered API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-api-details.md): __All products__ Returns details about an API discovered in account traffic. It is identified based on hostname and basepath.
- [List discovered APIs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-api-list.md): __All products__ List APIs discovered in your traffic that are new or not yet protected under API protections. An API is considered new if it isn't registered, and appears in the discovered APIs list if it's had at least 1000 requests over the last 30 days and at least one 2xx response.
- [List discovered API endpoints](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-discovered-api-endpoints.md): __All products__ Returns API endpoints for the specified hostname and base path. Unlike [Get discovered APIs](ref:get-api-list), this operation returns APIs already in your API definitions. When you see a discovered API or a new resource, add it to your definition with [Create an endpoint or resource](ref:post-api-endpoint-resource).
- [Create an endpoint or resource](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-api-endpoint-resource.md): __All products__ Create a new API endpoint or add a resource to an existing endpoint. When you run this operation to add a new resource to an existing API, the API version is cloned for you and the resource is included in the new version.
- [Modify an API's visibility](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/put-api-visibility.md): __All products__ Hide or show an API. You can choose to include or exclude APIs from the [Get discovered APIs](ref:get-api-list) response. This operation is per-user. If you hide an API from the response, another of your colleagues will still see that API in the response when they run this operation with their own credentials.
- [Get hostname coverage](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-hostname-coverage.md): __Kona Site Defender__, __App & API Protector with the Advanced Security module__ Get the list of hostnames in the account with their current protections, activation statuses, and other summary information.
- [List subscribers](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/get-subscription-feature.md): __Kona Site Defender__ List all subscribers to a security configuration's `feature`. The response array is empty if there are no subscribers. Currently available `feature` values are `AAG_TUNING_REC` for AAG and ASE rule sets, and `WAF_RULE_UPDATE` for ASE rule sets.
- [Subscribe or unsubscribe to recommendation emails](https://techdocs.akamai.com/application-security/reference/post-subscription-feature.md): __Kona Site Defender__ Set the request object's `action` to `subscribe` to add user emails to the subscription object. Use `unsubscribe` to remove them from the list. The lists are organized by `features`. Currently available `feature` values are `AAG_TUNING_REC` for AAG and ASE rule sets, and `WAF_RULE_UPDATE` for ASE rule sets.
