# Enterprise Application Access API Documentation

## API Reference
- [List admin event reports](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-adminevents-reports.md): Lists admin event reports with optional custom filters.
- [API concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/api-concepts.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/api-summary.md)
- [Enterprise Application Access API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/api.md)
- [Connector package types](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/connector-package-types.md)
- [Directory service configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/directory-configuration.md)
- [Enumerations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/enumerations.md)
- [Rule types & actions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/rule-types-actions.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/404.md)
- [500](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/500.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/errors.md)
- [Application creation workflow](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/application-creation-workflow.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-started.md)
- [Rate Limiting](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/resource-limiting.md)
- [Timestamp formats](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/timestamp-formats.md)
- [List application audit logs reports](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-application-reports.md): Lists an audit log report with optional custom filters.
- [Delete an application bundle](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-appbundle.md): Removes an application bundle.
- [Get an application bundle](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-appbundle.md): Lists details for a specific application bundle by ID.
- [List application bundles](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-appbundles.md): Lists your application bundles.
- [Deploy an application bundle](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-appbundle-deploy.md): Deploys the specified application bundle.
- [Create an application bundle](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-appbundle.md): Creates a new application bundle.
- [Modify an application bundle](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-appbundle.md): Modifies an existing application bundle.
- [Delete an application category](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-app-category.md): Removes an application category from your Enterprise Application Access product.
- [List application categories](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-categories.md): Lists your application categories.
- [Get an application category](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-category.md): Lists details for a specific application category.
- [Create an application category](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-category.md): Create a new application category.
- [Modify an application category](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-app-category.md): Modifies an existing application category.
- [List application directories](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-directories.md): Lists your application directories.
- [Get an application directory](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-directory.md): Get details for a specific application directory.
- [Create or delete application directories](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-directory.md): Creates or deletes a set of application and directories.
- [Get an application group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-group.md): Get details for an application group.
- [Assign directory groups to applications](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-assign-app-groups.md): Assigns one or more directory groups to one or more applications.
- [Delete an application IDP](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-app-idp.md): Removes the IDP assigned to your application.
- [Get an application IDP](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-idp.md): Get details for a specific application IDP.
- [List application IDPs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-idps.md): Lists your application IDPs.
- [Assign an IDP to an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-idp.md): Assign an existing IDP to an application. Overwrites any existing IDP assigned to the application.
- [List application rewrite groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-groups.md): Lists your application groups.
- [List the groups assigned to an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-groups-per-app.md): Lists the groups authorized to access the application.
- [Delete an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-app.md): Removes an application.
- [Get an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app.md): Lists a specified application.
- [List applications](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-apps.md): Lists your EAA applications.
- [Deploy an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-deploy.md): Deploys an application.
- [Create an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app.md): Creates a new application. After successful creation, additional steps are needed to deploy the application, depending on the application type. Refer to [Application creation workflow](ref:application-creation-workflow).
- [Modify an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-app.md): Updates the details of an application.
- [Block a user from accessing an IDP](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-user-block-idp-access.md): Blocks a user from accessing an IDP.
- [Get a connector for an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-connector.md): Lists connectors for an application.
- [List connectors for an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-connectors.md): Lists connectors for an application.
- [List application metrics for connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-connector-app-metrics.md): Lists application metrics for the specified connector.
- [Assign connectors to an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-connectors.md): Assign connectors to an application.
- [Create an edge key](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-edgekey.md): Generates an edge key for an application.
- [Create a Ghost to Origin key](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-g2o.md): Enables a G2O configuration for an application. Used only if you've enabled [Akamai Edge Enforcement](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa/docs/setup-advanced-settings-app).
- [List services for an application](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-services.md): Lists the services for an application.
- [Unblock a user from accessing an IDP](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-user-unblock-idp-access.md): Unblocks a user from accessing an IDP.
- [Delete a URL location rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-app-urllocation.md): Removes a URL location rule.
- [Get a URL location rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-urllocation.md): Gets the URL location rule for an application.
- [List URL location rules](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-urllocations.md): Lists URL location rules for an application.
- [Create a URL location rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-app-urllocation.md): Creates a URL location rule.
- [Modify a URL location rule](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-app-urllocation.md): Edits a URL location rule.
- [Get an application version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-version.md): Lists an application version.
- [List application versions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-app-versions.md): Lists application versions.
- [Create a new application version from existing](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-app-version.md): Copies the selected application version to a new draft application.
- [Delete a certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-certificate.md): Removes an existing certificate.
- [Get a certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-certificate.md): Retrieves a single certificate by ID.
- [List CA certificate attributes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-certificates-ca-attributes.md): Lists CA attributes.
- [List certificates](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-certificates.md): Lists certificates.
- [Upload a certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-certificate-upload.md): Modifies a certificate.
- [Create a certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-certificate.md): Creates a new certificate.
- [Modify a manual certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-certificate.md): Updates an existing manual certificate.
- [Delete a connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-connector.md): Deletes an existing connector.
- [Get a connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-connector.md): Retrieves a single connector by ID.
- [List connectors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-connectors.md): Lists connectors.
- [Approve a connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-connector-approve.md): Approves a connector.
- [Create a connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-connector.md): Creates a new connector.
- [Upgrade OS packages on a connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/post-upgrade-connector.md): Upgrades an existing connector.
- [Modify a connector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-connector.md): Updates an existing connector.
- [List connector system metrics](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-connector-system-metrics.md): Lists system metrics for the specified connector.
- [Get device history](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-device-history.md): Lists device history for a particular device.
- [List anti-malware status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-anti-malware-status.md): Lists anti-malware status signals metrics acquired from device application access. The response groups statuses by `value` and sums by `count`.
- [List devices with biometrics](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-device-biometric.md): Lists activity metrics for biometrics signals acquired from devices accessing your applications. The response groups statuses by `value` and sums by `count`.
- [List jailbroken devices](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-device-jailbroken.md): Lists jailbroken device signal metrics acquired from device application access. The response groups statuses by `value` and sums by `count`.
- [List device inventory](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-device-posture-inventory.md): Lists device inventories.
- [List device activity](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-device-roll-ups.md): Lists activity metrics of devices connecting  to your applications.
- [List device screen lock](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-device-screen-lock.md): Lists device screen lock signal metrics acquired from device application access. The response groups statuses by `value` and sums by `count`.
- [List system disk encryption](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-disk-encryption.md): Lists activity metrics for disk encryption signals acquired from devices accessing your applications. The response groups statuses by `value` and sums by `count`.
- [List firewall status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-firewall-status.md): Lists activity metrics for firewall status signals acquired from devices accessing your applications. The response groups statuses by `value` and sums by `count`.
- [List device risk assessment](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-risk-assessment.md): Lists activity metrics for risk assessment rules of devices connected to your applications.
- [List browser activity](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-summary-browsers.md): Lists installed browser activity metrics for devices connecting to your applications.
- [List operating system activity](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-summary-os.md): Lists activity metrics for installed operating  systems of devices connecting to your applications.
- [Update device status to clear](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/put-device-clear.md): Marks a device as safe and clear from ETP threats.
- [Delete an anti-malware profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-anti-malware-profile.md): Delete an anti-malware profile.
- [Delete certificate profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-certificate-profile.md): Delete a certificate profile.
- [Delete a Carbon Black integration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-partner-carbon-black-integration.md): Deletes a specific Carbon Black integration.
- [Delete a CrowdStrike integration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-partner-crowdstrike-integration.md): Deletes a specific CrowdStrike integration.
- [Delete a tag](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/delete-tag.md): Removes a risk tag from your device posture configuration.
- [Get an anti-malware profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-anti-malware-profile.md): Get an anti-malware profile.
- [List anti-malware profiles](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-anti-malware-profiles.md): Lists the existing anti-malware profiles.
- [List available browser versions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/eaa-api/reference/get-available-browsers.md): Lists supported browser versions for risk assessment. Unlisted browsers aren't supported for risk assessment.

- [Get an event](https://techdocs.akamai.com/event-ctr/reference/get-event.md): Returns details of the specified event.
- [List events](https://techdocs.akamai.com/event-ctr/reference/get-events.md): Returns a list of events for the current account and contract type. This call supports paging and sorting of the result data.
- [Create an event](https://techdocs.akamai.com/event-ctr/reference/post-events.md): Creates an event with the specified parameters.
- [Update an event](https://techdocs.akamai.com/event-ctr/reference/put-event.md): Updates the specified event.
- [List tags](https://techdocs.akamai.com/event-ctr/reference/get-tags.md): Returns a list of all event tags for the current account and contract type. This call supports paging and sorting of the result data.

## Recipes

## API Reference
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/404.md)
- [405](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/405.md)
- [415](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/415.md)
- [500](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/500.md)
- [502](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/502.md)
- [503](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/503.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/api-errors.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/api-get-started.md)
- [Firewall Rules Notification API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/api.md)
- [Concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/concepts.md)
- [Rate limits](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/rate-limiting.md)
- [List CIDR blocks](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/get-cidr-blocks.md): List all CIDR blocks for all services you are subscribed to. To see additional CIDR blocks, subscribe yourself to more services and run this operation again.
- [List services](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/get-services.md): Get a list of services you are subscribed to. To see additional services, subscribe to them first and run the operation again.
- [Get a service](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/get-service.md): Get information about a specific service.
- [List subscriptions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/get-subscriptions.md): List subscriptions you created for yourself and other users. You can only see the subscription information for configurations you created yourself. If someone else subscribed you to a service, you won't see that subscription returned by this operation.
- [Add or remove subscriptions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/patch-subscriptions.md): Subscribe or unsubscribe users to services.
- [Update subscriptions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/firewall-rules/reference/put-subscriptions.md): Subscribe or unsubscribe users to services.

- [Domain object](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/domain-object.md)

- [Global Traffic Management API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/api.md)
- [Best practices interacting with GTM API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/best-practices-interacting-with-gtm-api.md)
- [Data conventions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/data-conventions.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-started.md)
- [Remove an AS map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/delete-as-map.md): Removes a specific AS map.
- [Get an AS map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-as-map.md): Returns information for a specific AS map.
- [List AS maps](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-as-maps.md): Returns a list of AS maps.
- [Create or update an AS map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-as-map.md): Run this operation to create or update an AS map.
- [Remove a CIDR map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/delete-cidr-map.md): Removes a specific CIDR map.
- [Get a CIDR map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-cidr-map.md): Returns information for a specific CIDR map.
- [List CIDR maps](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-cidr-maps.md): Returns a list of CIDR maps.
- [Create or update a CIDR map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-cidr-map.md): Run this operation to create or update a CIDR map.
- [List Akamai cloud regions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-akamai-cloud-regions.md): This operation returns a list of available Akamai cloud regions. Use these regions when creating a data center with a `cloudProvider` of `akamai-cloud`. Each region includes geographic metadata such as city, country, latitude, and longitude that you can use to populate data center fields.
- [List datacenter IDs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-datacenter-ids.md): Returns a paginated list of valid datacenter IDs available for use as virtual datacenters.
- [List DNS record set types](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-recordset-types.md): Returns the DNS record types supported for GTM properties, optionally filtered by domain.
- [List supported geo codes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-supported-geo-codes.md): Returns the full hierarchy of supported geographic codes — continents, countries, and states or provinces — that you can use when configuring geographic maps.
- [Remove a data center](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/delete-datacenter.md): Remove a data center: This operation removes a data center. You can only remove a data center when no properties or resources are assigned to it.
- [Get a data center](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-datacenter.md): This operation returns information for a specific data center.
- [List data centers](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-datacenters.md): This operation returns a list of data centers.
- [Create an IPv4 data center for ip-version-selector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/post-datacenter-for-ipv.md): This operation creates a data center which is solely reserved for use by properties of the IP Version Selector type `qtr`. The data center created by this resource  is assigned the `datacenterId` 5401, which is interpreted as the target for `A` record requests.
- [Create an IPv6 data center for ip-version-selector](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/post-datacenter-for-ipv6.md): This operation creates a data center which is solely reserved for use by properties of type `qtr` (IP Version Selector). The data center created by this resource  is assigned the `datacenterId` 5402, which is interpreted as the target for `AAAA` record requests.
- [Create a data center](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/post-datacenter.md): This operation creates a specific data center.
- [Create a default data center](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/post-default-datacenter-for-maps.md): This operation creates a data center for use by the map-type properties CIDR, Geographic or AS mapping as the default data center.
- [Update a data center](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-datacenter.md): This operation updates a specific data center.
- [Get domain authorities](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domain-authorities.md): Returns authorities for a specific GTM domain if it is a custom domain and not hosted on config-gtm.
- [Get delete request result](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domain-delete-request-result.md): Returns the final result of a completed bulk domain delete request, listing which domains were successfully deleted and which failed. Only available once `isComplete` is `true` in the status endpoint.
- [Get delete request status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domain-delete-request.md): Returns the current status of a bulk domain delete request, including how many domains have been successfully deleted, failed, and whether processing is complete.
- [Get domain features](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domain-features.md): Returns the contract features available for a specific GTM domain, including the set of supported property types and security features such as DNSSEC.
- [List domain history](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domain-history.md): Returns a list of each change made to the domain.
- [Get a domain summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domain-summary.md): Returns summary information for a specific GTM domain, including its activation state, last modification details, and DNSSEC configuration.
- [Get a domain](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domain.md): Returns information for a specific GTM domain.
- [List domains](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-domains.md): Returns a list of GTM domain summaries under your contract.
- [Submit a bulk domain delete request](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/post-domain-delete-requests.md): Submits an asynchronous request to delete one or more GTM domains. Poll the returned `requestId` using the status endpoint to track progress.
- [Create a domain](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/post-domain.md): Creates a new GTM domain.
- [Update a domain](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-domain.md): Updates a specific GTM domain.
- [Remove a geographic map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/delete-geographic-map.md): Removes a geographic map.
- [Get a geographic map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-geographic-map.md): Returns information for a specific geographic map.
- [List geographic maps](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-geographic-maps.md): Returns a list of geographic maps.
- [Create or update a geographic map](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-geographic-map.md): Run this operation to create or update a geographic map.
- [Datacenter](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/datacenters-versions.md)
- [Domain summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/domain-summary.md)
- [Domain](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/domain-versions.md)
- [Liveness test](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/liveness-test.md)
- [Property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/property.md)
- [Traffic target](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/traffic-target.md)
- [List contracts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-identity-contracts.md): This operation returns a list of API client `contracts`. When you [Create a domain](ref:post-domain), you may need a `contractId` under certain circumstances. Run this operation to view available values.
- [List groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-identity-groups.md): This operation returns a list of API client `groups` and their parameters.  When you [Create a domain](ref:post-domain), you may need a `groupId` under certain circumstances. Run this operation to view available values.
- [Get identity](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-identity.md): This operation returns information about the API client. Run this operation for details on the API client contract such as available load balancing `features` and `permissions` the client has on the domains tied to the contract.
- [Remove a property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/delete-property.md): Removes a specific GTM property.
- [List properties](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-properties.md): Returns a list of GTM properties.
- [Get a property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-property.md): Returns information for a specific GTM property.
- [Add or update properties](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/patch-properties.md): Adds or updates properties on a domain without replacing the entire set. Properties not included in the request body are left unchanged.
- [Replace all properties](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-properties.md): Replaces the complete set of properties on a domain with those specified in the request body.
- [Create or update a property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-property.md): Run this operation to create or update a GTM property.
- [Remove a resource](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/delete-resource.md): Removes a specific resource.
- [Get a resource](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-resource.md): Returns information for a specific resource.
- [List resources](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-resources.md): Returns a list of resources created within the domain that constrain one or more properties in a data center.
- [Create or update a resource](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/put-resource.md): Run this operation to create or update a resource.
- [Get current status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-status-current.md): This operation returns information about the current condition of the change in the domain's activation.
- [Get status for a domain version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm/reference/get-status.md): Returns the propagation status for a specific domain version, identified by its `changeId`.

# GTM Load Feedback API Documentation

## API Reference
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/api-summary.md)
- [API workflow](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/api-workflow.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/400.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/404.md)
- [405](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/405.md)
- [429](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/429.md)
- [500](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/500.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/errors.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/get-started.md)
- [Global Traffic Management Load Feedback API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/global-traffic-management-load-feedback-api.md)
- [Resources](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/resources.md)
- [Get load data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/get-domain-resource.md): Use this action to fetch the load data for a resource instance. The URL format is the same as the POST action. The API returns either XML or JSON, depending on your `Accept` header. If both are specified, or there is no `Accept` header, the default is JSON. In this sample data, we're fetching load for a resource called `connections` in datacenter 100, in the `example.akadns.net` domain.
- [Submit load data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-load-feedback/reference/post-domain-resource.md): Use this action to submit load data. The URL specifies the `domain`, `resource`, and `datacenterId` of the instance to which the load data pertains. The data may be submitted in either XML or JSON format. To support legacy clients, we allow `region` as an alias for `datacenterId`. The timestamp string should be in XSD format.

# GTM Reporting API Documentation

## API Reference
- [API concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/api-concepts.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/api-summary.md)
- [Global Traffic Management Reporting API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/api.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/404.md)
- [405](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/405.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/errors.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-started.md)
- [Report directed demand for a property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-demand-property.md): The requests generated by the client machines behind a client name-server represent demand. This API returns a collection of reports containing the provisioned demand percentage, along with actual demand percentage seen by GTM rounded off to nearest 5 min interval for each data center. This API returns no data for load feedback-based domains, as those domains do not have a configured per data center load.
- [Get a directed demand report window](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-demand-window.md): Returns the date range of data available for directed demand reports. Use the returned `start` and `end` values as the bounds for queries to the directed demand report endpoint.
- [Report datacenter diagnostics](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-diagnostics-datacenter.md): Returns diagnostic data showing how traffic from autonomous systems and countries is being distributed across datacenters for the given domain and time period.
- [Report IP diagnostics](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-diagnostics-ip.md): Returns diagnostic data showing the per-name server mapping decisions made by GTM for the given domain, property, IP address, and time period. Includes scoring details such as latency, ping scores, and which datacenter was selected.
- [Report detailed map diff diagnostics for a data center and AS](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-diagnostics-mapdiff-datacenter-as.md): Returns detailed geo and ping score time series for the given domain, datacenter, and autonomous system over the specified time period.
- [Report detailed map diff diagnostics for a data center and country](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-diagnostics-mapdiff-datacenter-country.md): Returns detailed geo and ping score time series for the given domain, datacenter, and country over the specified time period.
- [Report map diff diagnostics](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-diagnostics-mapdiff.md): Returns a diff of traffic distribution map snapshots for the given domain and time period, showing how AS and country-to-datacenter mappings changed between consecutive five-minute intervals.
- [Get a domain summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-domain-list-domain.md): This operation retrieves a list of property names, as well as Datacenter names and IDs for a given domain.
- [Get a domain list](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-domain-list.md): This operation retrieves a list of domains, along with a URL templated link to obtain a domain summary of associated properties and data centers for each.
- [List domain names for an account and contract type](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-domain-names-for-account.md): Returns a list of GTM domain names accessible to the current user for the given account and contract type.
- [List domain names](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-domain-names.md): Returns a list of GTM domain names accessible to the current user.
- [List IP availability per property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-ip-availability-property.md): GTM combines scores (based on Liveness Tests) from all Web agents to calculate Server Scores and makes liveness decisions. A cutoff value is computed for each property from the median scores for all the servers in the property. Any property server with a score over the cutoff value is considered dead and it won't be handed out. The request should have either start and end time or mostRecent, and not both.  In the response, `handedOut` in JSON or `handOut` in CSV indicates whether the server was handed out and alive indicates whether GTM considered the server as alive or not.
- [Report latency per data center](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-latency-datacenter.md): This operation returns a collection of reports representing latency and loss of ping data as reported by network agents in each Data Center for a property. Latency and Loss parameters follow bucket ranges schema (bucket ranges, delimited by a pipe: `|`).  Ping Loss ranges from `0%` to `100%`. So a schema of `5|10|15|20` would represent grouping of data from `0%` to `5%`, `5%` to `10%`, `10%` to `15%`, `15%` to `20%`, and `20%` to `100%` bucket ranges. The end of one bucket would be the beginning of the next bucket. Similarly `0|5|80` would represent `0%`, `0%` to `5%`, `5%` to `100%`, and `80%` to `100%` bucket ranges. A trailing pipe at the end of a bucket indicates that there is no _greater than the max_ bucket. That is, in the above example if loss schema were `0|5|80|` then it would mean `0%`, `0%` to `5%`, and `5%` to `80%`.  Latency is measured in milliseconds. Each bucket ranges from 0 ms to 1000 ms.  So, a schema of `5|10|300|1000` would mean `0ms` to `5ms`, `5ms` to `10ms`, `10ms` to `300ms`, `300ms` to `1000ms`, and `> 1000ms`.
- [Get a Liveness Test error](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-liveness-tests-error-code.md): This operation returns an error description for the given Liveness Test error ID.
- [List liveness test errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-liveness-tests-error-codes.md): This operation lists all the possible liveness test error codes (as displayed on the Liveness Test Failures report) and a description of each error.
- [Report liveness per property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-liveness-tests-property.md): This operation returns a collection of reports containing logs of individual liveness tests which have failed. Liveness Tests are run periodically by Web Agents installed in customer data centers. Liveness scores are used by GTM to decide whether a data center is alive or not.  Liveness tests which fail in succession (tests that run from the same testing agent to the same origin server, which fail with the same error code) are grouped together and represented with a duration. This duration value is in seconds and can sustain for up to 86400 seconds (one day). If it crosses that boundary, GTM logs it as a new failure at the beginning of the next day (00:00 UTC), with the duration reset to that point.
- [Get a liveness test report window](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-liveness-tests-window.md): Returns the date range of data available for liveness test reports. Use the returned `start` and `end` values as the bounds for queries to the liveness test report endpoint.
- [Get a load feedback report window](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-load-feedback-window.md): Returns the date range of data available for load feedback reports. Use the returned `start` and `end` values as the bounds for queries to the load feedback report endpoint.
- [Report load feedback](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-load-feedback.md): This report shows the historical values of a given Resource across all data centers and the given time period. A resource is anything that can constrain the load sent to a data center. Examples of typical resources include bandwidth, CPU load average, database queries per second, or disk operations per second.
- [Get most recent load feedback data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-most-recent-load-data.md): Returns the most recent load feedback values reported by each data center for the given domain. This provides a snapshot of the current load state across all data centers.
- [Report traffic per data center](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-traffic-datacenter.md): This report shows, for a given domain and data center, the DNS requests received in each five-minute period.  Requests are broken down by property.
- [Get a datacenter traffic report window](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-traffic-datacenters-window.md): Returns the date range of data available for datacenter traffic reports. Use the returned `start` and `end` values as the bounds for queries to the datacenter traffic report endpoint.
- [Get a property traffic report window](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-traffic-properties-window.md): Returns the date range of data available for property traffic reports. Use the returned `start` and `end` values as the bounds for queries to the property traffic report endpoint.
- [Report traffic per property](https://techdocs.akamai.com/gtm-reporting/reference/get-traffic-property.md): This report shows, for a given domain and property, the DNS requests received in each five-minute period.  Requests are broken down by data center.

# Identity and Access Management API Documentation

## API Reference
- [Remove your credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-self-credential.md): This operation deletes a specific credential from your API client. You can only delete inactive credentials.
- [Get your credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-self-credential.md): This operation provides details about a specific credential for your API client. To change the credential's expiration date or toggle its activation status, run the [Update a credential](ref:put-client-credential) operation.
- [List your credentials](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-self-credentials.md): This operation lists your API client's credentials.
- [Deactivate your credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-self-credential-deactivate.md): This operation deactivates a specific credential for your API client. This doesn't delete the client or the credentials. To update a specific credential, run the [Update your credential](ref:put-self-credential) operation.
- [Deactivate your credentials](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-self-credentials-deactivate.md): This operation deactivates all credentials for your API client. This doesn't delete the API client or the credentials. To deactivate a single credential, run either the [Deactivate your credential](ref:post-self-credential-deactivate) operation or [Update your credential](ref:put-self-credential) and set the status to `INACTIVE`.
- [Create your credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-self-credentials.md): This operation creates a new credential for your API client. Only you may create credentials for this client. Credentials are in `active` status at creation. By default, they expire two years from their creation date. However, when the API client's permissions are based on Control Center permissions, credentials expire differently and follow the same rotation schedule listed for user passwords on those accounts. Run the [Update a credential](ref:put-client-credential) operation to change the expiration date, description, or status. Save the values from the response, such as the `credentialID` for future use. This is the only time you'll see the client secret. Save the credential at this time to avoid the need to create a new one.
- [Update your credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-self-credential.md): This operation updates a specific credential for your API client. You can change the expiration date, description, or toggle the activation status. This isn't the same as rotating a credential. For details, see [Rotate credentials](ref:rotate-credentials).
- [Remove a credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-client-credential.md): This operation deletes a specific credential from an API client. You can only delete inactive credentials.
- [Get a credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-client-credential.md): This operation returns details about a specific credential for an API client. To change the credential's expiration date or toggle its activation status, run the [Update credential for an API client](ref:put-client-credential) operation.
- [List credentials](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-client-credentials.md): This operation lists credentials for an API client.
- [Deactivate a credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-client-credential-deactivate.md): This operation deactivates a specific credential for an API client. This doesn't delete the API client, or the other credentials.
- [Deactivate credentials](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-client-credentials-deactivate.md): This operation deactivates all credentials for a specific API client. This doesn't delete the API client or the credentials. To deactivate a single credential, run either the [Deactivate a credential](ref:post-client-credential-deactivate) operation or [Update a credential](ref:put-client-credential) and set the status to `INACTIVE`.
- [Create a credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-client-credentials.md): This operation creates a new credential for your API client. If you don't know your `clientId`, you can run the [Create your credential](ref:post-self-credentials) operation. Credentials are in `active` status at creation. By default, they expire two years from their creation date. However, when the API client's permissions are based on Control Center permissions, credentials expire differently and follow the same rotation schedule listed for user passwords on those accounts. Run the [Update a credential](ref:put-client-credential) operation to change the expiration date, description, or status. Save the values from the response, such as the `credentialID`, for future use. This is the only time you'll see the client secret. Save the credential to avoid the need to create a new one.
- [Update a credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-client-credential.md): This operation updates a specific credential for an API client. You can change the expiration date, description, or toggle the activation status. This isn't the same as rotating a credential. For details, see [Rotate credentials](ref:rotate-credentials).
- [Delete your API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-api-client-self.md): This operation permanently deletes an API client you own, without active credentials. It also breaks any API connections with the client. Before deleting your API client, deactivate all credentials and make sure there aren't any API integrations or scripts using them.
- [Get your API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-api-client-self.md): This operation provides details about your API client.
- [List your account switch keys](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-self-account-switch-keys.md): This operation lists account switch keys available for your API client. You can use the `accountSwitchKey` in many Akamai APIs [to make an API call to another account](https://techdocs.akamai.com/developer/docs/manage-many-accounts-with-one-api-client).
- [Update your API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-api-clients-self.md): This operation updates your API client.
- [Lock your API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-lock-api-client-self.md): This operation locks your API client, preventing it from making requests on the network. You can't use the API client while it's locked. To unlock your client, run the [Unlock an API client](ref:put-unlock-api-client) operation.
- [Delete an API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-api-client.md): This operation permanently deletes the API client, breaking any API connections with the client. To delete a client, you need to own the client, or have admin access on a given group. Before deleting an API client, make sure there aren't any API integrations or scripts using it.
- [Get an API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-api-client.md): This operation provides details about an API client.
- [List API clients](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-api-clients.md): This operation lists API clients an administrator can manage on the current account or other managed accounts using the `accountSwitchKey` parameter.
- [List account switch keys](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-client-account-switch-keys.md): This operation lists account switch keys available for a specific API client. The client can use the `accountSwitchKey` in many Akamai APIs to [make an API call to another account](https://techdocs.akamai.com/developer/docs/manage-many-accounts-with-one-api-client).
- [Create an API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-api-clients.md): This operation creates a new API client. Optionally, you can automatically assign a credential for the client when you create it. If you choose not to assign the credential automatically, see [Create authentication credentials](https://techdocs.akamai.com/developer/docs/set-up-authentication-credentials) for details. This operation doesn't support service accounts. To create or manage API clients for service accounts, you need to use [Identity and Access interface](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam/docs/create-an-api-client-for-a-service-account).
- [Update an API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-api-clients.md): This operation updates an API client. This operation doesn't support service accounts. To create or manage API clients for service accounts, you need to use [Identity and Access interface](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam/docs/create-an-api-client-for-a-service-account).
- [Lock an API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-lock-api-client.md): This operation locks an API client, preventing it from making requests on the network. You can't use the API client while it's locked. To unlock a client, run the [Unlock an API client](ref:put-unlock-api-client) operation.
- [Unlock an API client](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-unlock-api-client.md): This operation unlocks an API client, allowing it to make requests on the network.
- [List allowed APIs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-api-clients-users-allowed-apis.md): This operation lists available APIs for a user.
- [List accessible groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-api-clients-users-group-access.md): This operation lists groups available to a user.
- [List authorized users](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-api-clients-users.md): This operation lists authorized API client users.
- [List allowed CP codes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-api-clients-users-allowed-cpcodes.md): This operation lists available CP codes for a user.
- [Identity and Access Management concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/api-concepts.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/404.md)
- [405](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/405.md)
- [415](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/415.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/api-errors.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/api-summary.md)
- [Identity and Access Management API v3](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/api.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-started.md)
- [Identity and Access Management migration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/iam-api-migration.md)
- [Manage access to properties and includes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/manage-access-to-properties-and-includes.md)
- [Rate limits](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/rate-limits.md)
- [Rotate credentials](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/rotate-credentials.md)
- [View contact types](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-contact-types.md): This operation lists the supported contact types. Administrators should use the values from this operation to add or update a user's `contactType`. Users should run the [View contact types for a user profile](ref:get-user-contact-types) operation before modifying their contact type.
- [View countries](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-countries.md): This operation lists the supported countries. Administrators should use the values from this operation to add or update a user's `country`. Users should run the [View supported countries for a user profile](ref:get-user-countries) operation before modifying their country.
- [View languages](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-languages.md): This operation lists the supported languages. Administrators should use the values from this operation to set a user's `preferredLanguage`. Users should run the [View languages for a user profile](ref:get-user-languages) operation before setting their preferred language.
- [View states](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-states.md): This operation lists the supported U.S. states or Canadian provinces. Administrators should use the values from this operation to add or update a user's `state`. If a user's state or province is unknown, use `TBD`. Users should run the [View states for a user profile](ref:get-user-states) operation before modifying their state.
- [View time zones](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-timezones.md): This operation lists the supported time zones in the ISO 8601 format. Administrators should use the values from this operation to set a user's `timeZone`. Users should run the [View time zones for a user profile](ref:get-user-timezones) operation before setting their time zone.
- [View password policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-password-policy.md): This operation lists the password policy for the account. The tokens in your API client determine the account. Administrators should use the values from this operation to set a user's password policy. Users should run the [View password policy for a user profile](ref:get-user-password-policy) operation before setting their password policy.
- [View timeout policies](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-timeout-policies.md): This operation returns the supported session timeout policies an administrator can use when [creating](ref:post-ui-identity) and [updating](ref:put-ui-identity-basic-info) user profiles. The `name` for each timeout period is in minutes, and the time `value` is in seconds. Make sure to select a value that's equal or smaller to the **Max session timeout** value, set for your account in the Identity and Access Management interface's [Settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam/docs/account-settings) tab. Users should run the [View timeout policies for a user profile](ref:get-user-timeout-policies) operation before setting their timeout policy.
- [View products](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-common-notification-products.md): This operation lists the products a user can subscribe to and receive notifications for on the account. The tokens in your API client determine the account. Administrators should use the values from this operation to set a user's product notifications. Users should run the [View products for a user profile](ref:get-user-notification-products) operation before setting their product notifications.
- [View contact types for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-contact-types.md): This operation lists the supported contact types. Users should use the values from this operation to add or update their `contactType`. Administrators should run the [View contact types](ref:get-common-contact-types) operation before modifying a user's contact type.
- [View supported countries for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-countries.md): This operation lists the supported countries. Users should use the values from this operation to add or update their `country`. Administrators should run the [View supported countries](ref:get-common-countries) operation before modifying a user's country.
- [View languages for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-languages.md): This operation lists the supported languages. Users should use the values from this operation to set their preferred language. Administrators should run the [View languages](ref:get-common-languages) operation before setting a user's preferred language.
- [View states for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-states.md): This operation lists the supported U.S. states or Canadian provinces. Users should use the values from this operation to add or update their `state`. Administrators should run the [View states](ref:get-common-states) operation before modifying a user's state.
- [View time zones for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-timezones.md): This operation lists the supported time zones in the ISO 8601 format. Users should use the values from this operation to set their `timeZone`. Administrators should run the [View time zones](ref:get-common-timezones) operation before setting a user's time zone.
- [View password policy for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-password-policy.md): This operation lists the password policy for the account. The account is determined by the tokens in your API client. Users should use the values from this operation to set their password policy.  Administrators should run the [View password policy](ref:get-common-password-policy) operation before setting a user's password policy.
- [View timeout policies for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-timeout-policies.md): This operation returns the supported session timeout policies an account user can use when [updating](ref:put-user-profile-info) their profile. The `name` for each timeout period is in minutes, and the time `value` is in seconds. Make sure to select a value that's equal or smaller to the **Max session timeout** value, set for your account in the Identity and Access Management interface's [Settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam/docs/account-settings) tab. Administrators should run the [View timeout policies](ref:get-common-timeout-policies) operation before setting a user's timeout policy.
- [View products for a user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-notification-products.md): This operation lists the products a user can subscribe to and receive notifications for on the account. The account is determined by the tokens in your API client. Users should use the values from this operation to set their product notifications. Administrators should run the [View products](ref:get-common-notification-products) operation before setting a user's product notifications.
- [Delete a group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-group.md): This operation deletes a group. You can't delete a group that contains subgroups, resources, or users with roles on that group. You need admin access to delete a group.
- [Get a group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-group.md): This operation retrieves a group's details.
- [List groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-groups.md): This operation lists all groups the administrator can manage for the current account and contract type or other managed accounts using the `accountSwitchKey` parameter. The account and contract type are determined by the access tokens in your API client.
- [Create a new group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-group.md): This operation creates a new group within the specified parent group.
- [Modify a group's name](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-group.md): This operation changes a group's name.
- [List users affected by moving a group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-move-affected-users.md): This operation lists users who are affected when you move a group. Users may lose or gain access to resources depending on the roles and permissions inherited from the new parent group. Users with a `userType` of `lostAccess` lose their access to the source group. If the `userType` is `gainAccess`, they gain access to the resources in the source group. Users who have inherited access to a group lose access to that group if it moves out of its hierarchy that gives them those access rights. If the group moves to another parent group to which they have access, they still have access to the group you move. Likewise, when you move a group to a new location, users who inherit their access rights from the new parent group gain access to the resources in the group you move.
- [Move a group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-groups-move.md): This operation moves a nested group under another group within the same parent hierarchy.
- [Delete a CIDR block](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-allowlist-cidrblockid.md): This operation deletes a CIDR block from the IP allowlist. After you delete a CIDR block, any requests from IP addresses you deleted fail the next time someone tries to log in from that address. Users accessing Control Center from an IP address you delete aren't automatically logged out of Control Center at the time you delete the IP address. With IP allowlist enabled for the account, you can't delete the last CIDR block in the allowlist or the CIDR block allowing access to the current user. There are no restrictions with IP allowlist disabled on the account.
- [Get a CIDR block](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-allowlist-cidrblockid.md): This operation retrieves a CIDR block's details.
- [Validate CIDR blocks](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-allowlist-validate.md): This operation checks the format of a potential CIDR block. If it's validated successfully, you can [create it](ref:post-allowlist).
- [List CIDR blocks](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-allowlist.md): This operation lists all CIDR blocks on your current account's allowlist or other managed accounts using the `accountSwitchKey` parameter. Your API client designates the allowlist for the account.
- [Create a CIDR block](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-allowlist.md): This operation adds CIDR blocks to your account's allowlist. You can add only one CIDR block at a time. Before creating the CIDR block, you may want to [validate it](ref:get-allowlist-validate), to check if its format is correct.
- [Modify a CIDR block](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-allowlist-cidrblockid.md): This operation enables or disables an existing CIDR block, updates comments, IP addresses, or ranges in the CIDR block. Note that you can only update a CIDR block with IP allowlist enabled on the account. There are several CIDR blocks on the allowlist and your attempt to make changes to the CIDR block is from an IP address that's on the allowlist. You can't edit the last CIDR block in the allowlist or the CIDR block allowing access to the current user.
- [View IP allowlist status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-allowlist-status.md): This operation indicates whether IP allowlist is enabled or disabled on your account.
- [Disable IP allowlist](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-allowlist-disable.md): This operation disables IP allowlist on your account. After you disable IP allowlist, users can access Control Center regardless of their IP address or who assigns it.
- [Enable IP allowlist](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-allowlist-enable.md): This operation enables IP allowlist on your account. Before you enable IP allowlist, add at least one IP address to allow access to Control Center. The allowlist can't be empty with IP allowlist enabled.
- [List properties or includes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-properties.md): This operation lists the properties and includes for the current account or other managed accounts using the `accountSwitchKey` parameter. Include the `groupId` parameter in your request to filter the results by group. The response returns a list of `propertyId` regardless of whether they represent a property or include in PAPI. To learn more, see [Manage access to properties and includes](ref:manage-access-to-properties-and-includes).
- [List users for property or include](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-property-users.md): This operation lists users who can access a property or include.
- [Get a property or include](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-property.md): This operation fetches a property's or include's details. The response returns details for `propertyId` regardless of whether it represent a property or include in PAPI. To learn more, see [Manage access to properties and includes](ref:manage-access-to-properties-and-includes).
- [Block users from a property or include](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-property-users-block.md): This operation blocks the users on a property or include.
- [Move a property or include](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-property.md): This operation moves a property or include from one group to another group. You can only move an asset into another group within the same group hierarchy. Depending on your role in the destination group, you may lose access to resources the asset uses. Before moving an asset, run the [List users affected by moving a group](ref:get-move-affected-users) operation to review which users will be affected by the move.
- [Get asset's resources](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-property-resources.md): This operation lists resources a property or include uses to deliver your web content across Akamai's edge network. You can think of these resources as objects, like CP codes and configuration files. For example, an ARL (Akamai resource locator) configuration file specifies how Akamai purges versions of an object that you can’t purge using just the URL.
- [List grantable roles](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-grantable-roles.md): This operation lists the grantable roles you can include in a new custom role, or add to an existing custom role.
- [Delete a role](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-role.md): This operation deletes a custom role. You can't delete a role if it's assigned to a user.
- [Get a role](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-role.md): This operation retrieves a role's details.
- [List roles](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-roles.md): This operation lists roles for the current account and contract type or other accounts using the `accountSwitchKey` parameter. The account and contract type are determined by the access tokens in your API client.
- [Create a role](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-role.md): This operation creates a custom role, which is a combination of [grantable roles](ref:get-grantable-roles). The role that you create becomes available only for your current contract type. To learn more, see [Identity and Access Management concepts](ref:api-concepts).
- [Edit a role](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-role.md): This operation adds or removes a role's group role assignments, along with other data such as name and description. When you modify a role, those changes affect existing API clients. Review all API clients belonging to the user before modifying a role.
- [List blocked properties and includes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-blocked-properties.md): This operation lists all properties and includes a user doesn't have access to in a group. Note the responses's set of IDs refer to either properties or includes. To find out which each refers to, see [Manage access to properties and includes](ref:manage-access-to-properties-and-includes).
- [Update blocked properties and includes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-blocked-properties.md): This operation updates the list of blocked properties and includes. By default, users can access all properties and includes in a group, and this operation specifies IDs for any to block. To find out which each refers to, see [Manage access to properties and includes](ref:manage-access-to-properties-and-includes). To get the list of already blocked properties run the [List blocked properties and includes](ref:get-blocked-properties) operation. In this request, include the whole list of properties you want to block. By not including a blocked property, you're unlocking it.
- [Set a user's multi-factor authentication](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-ui-identity-additional-authentication.md): This operation sets a user's multi-factor authentication to `TFA`, `MFA`, or `NONE`. Users are allowed five login attempts with MFA or 2FA before their account is locked. If the account is locked, use the [Reset additional authentication](ref:put-ui-identity-reset-additional-authentication) to unlock it.
- [Reset a user's multi-factor authentication](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-ui-identity-reset-additional-authentication.md): This operation prompts the reset of a user's multi-factor authentication method the next time they log in to Control Center. `TFA` indicates two-factor authentication. `MFA` indicates Akamai MFA authentication.
- [Reset a user's password](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-reset-password.md): This operation sends a one-time use password to the user. If you send the email with the password directly to the user, the response for this operation doesn't include that password. If you don't send the password to the user through email, the password is included in the response.
- [Set a user's password](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-set-password.md): This operation sets a password for a user. This differs from [Reset a user's password](ref:post-reset-password) because this password may be used more than once, and isn't randomly generated.
- [Lock a user's account](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-ui-identity-lock.md): This operation locks a user's account, preventing access to Control Center. To allow a user access to Control Center, run the [Unlock a user's account](ref:post-ui-identity-unlock) operation. To learn more, see [Temporary account lockout](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam/docs/tmp-account-lockout).
- [Unlock a user's account](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-ui-identity-unlock.md): This operation releases the lock on a user's account, allowing access to Control Center. To learn more, see [Temporary account lockout](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam/docs/tmp-account-lockout).
- [Modify a user's group and role assignments](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-ui-uiidentity-auth-grants.md): This operation edits what groups a user has access to, and how the user interacts with the objects in those groups.
- [Update a user's notifications](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-notifications.md): This operation subscribes or unsubscribes users to product notification emails.
- [Remove a user](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/delete-ui-identity.md): This operation deletes any user who doesn't own an API client. Before you delete a user with a client, [transfer API client ownership](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam/docs/transfer-api-client-ownership) to another user.
- [List users](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-ui-identities.md): This operation lists the users who have access to your current account or other managed accounts using the `accountSwitchKey` parameter. The account is determined by the tokens in your API client. You can pass a `groupId` to filter users based on group. You can also return user information such as what product notifications they subscribe to, or what group and role assignments they have. Set  the `actions` parameter to `true` to return the actions you can take on each user.
- [Get a user](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-ui-identity.md): This operation retrieves a user's profile.
- [Create a user](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-ui-identity.md): This operation creates a user, or clones an existing user's role assignments, in the account specified in your API client credentials. Optionally, sends a randomly generated one-time use password to the new user. If you send the email with the password directly to the user, the response for this operation doesn't include that password. If you don't send the password to the user through email, the password is included in the response.
- [Update a user](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-ui-identity-basic-info.md): This operation modifies a user's basic information. Pass the entire body of data in the request, including members you're not changing, or the unspecified data are removed. To edit detailed settings, run the [Modify a user's group and role assignments](ref:put-ui-uiidentity-auth-grants) or [Update a user's notifications](ref:put-notifications) operations.
- [View your profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/get-user-profile.md): This operation gets your profile information. To make changes to your profile, run the [Edit your profile](ref:put-user-profile-info) operation.
- [Edit your profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-user-profile-info.md): This operation updates your basic profile information, such as your name or phone number. To edit detailed settings, run the [Update your notifications](ref:put-user-profile-notifications) or [Modify a user's group and role assignments](ref:put-ui-uiidentity-auth-grants) operations.
- [Rotate your password](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/post-profile-change-password.md): This operation changes your password. Include your old password and your new password in the request body. Before you create your new password, run the [View password policy](ref:get-common-password-policy) operation to ensure it adheres to your policy. If you include your existing password incorrectly and make too many login attempts, your account locks.
- [Set multi-factor authentication for your user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-user-profile-additional-authentication.md): This operation sets multi-factor authentication to `TFA`, `MFA`, or `NONE`. You have five login attempts with MFA or 2FA before your account is locked. If your account is locked, use the [Reset multi-factor authentication for your user profile](ref:put-user-profile-reset-additional-authentication) operation to unlock it.
- [Reset multi-factor authentication for your user profile](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-user-profile-reset-additional-authentication.md): This operation resets your multi-factor authentication.
- [Update your notifications](https://techdocs.akamai.com/iam-api/reference/put-user-profile-notifications.md): This operation subscribes to notifications emails for password expiration reminders, proactive maintenance emails, and upgrade notification emails.

# Image and Video Manager Documentation

## API Reference
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/400.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/404.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/api-errors.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/api-summary.md)
- [Image and Video Manager API v2](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/api.md)
- [IVM Concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/concepts.md)
- [Enumeration values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/enum-values.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-started.md)
- [Rate limits](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/rate-limits.md)
- [Test images on-demand](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/test-images-on-demand.md)
- [Transformation types](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/transformation-types.md)
- [Workflow](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/workflow.md)
- [Delete a policy set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/delete-policyset.md): Delete a policy set from a contract on both networks.
- [Get a policy set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-policyset.md): View details for a specific policy set in production.
- [List policy sets](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-policysets.md): Returns a list of all policy sets for a contract in production.
- [Create a policy set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/post-policyset.md): Create a new policy set for the contract on both networks.
- [Update a policy set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/put-policyset.md): Update the `name` or `region` of an existing policy set on both networks.
- [Get an image](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-image.md): Returns a specific image.
- [List images](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-images.md): List a policy's images.
- [List error details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-errors.md): Returns a list of image or video errors for the transformations requested in the past three days.
- [List log details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-logs.md): Returns a list of image or video logs for the transformations requested in the past three days.
- [Remove a policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/delete-policy.md): Delete a specific policy.
- [List policies](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-policies.md): Returns a list of all policies belonging to the specified policy set.
- [Get a policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-policy.md): Returns a specific policy assigned to the specified policy set.
- [Create or modify a policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/put-policy.md): Run this operation to update an existing policy or create a new policy.  Run the [List policies](ref:get-policies) operation to get the list of existing policies and their policy IDs. Confirm the `policyId` doesn't already exist.
- [Roll back a policy](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/put-rollback-policy.md): Use this operation to revert the previous version of the policy and deploy it to the network. Use this operation to stop and reverse a policy rollout.
- [Get policy history](https://techdocs.akamai.com/ivm/reference/get-policy-history.md): Returns the policy history by policy ID. This operation returns the full state of the policy at various points in time. Time stamps are in ISO 8601 extended notation format.
