- [Allow access to an Object Storage bucket](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-object-storage-bucket-access.md): Apply basic cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) and [S3 canned access control list (ACL)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/acl-overview.html#canned-acl) settings.  > 📘 > > You can use the S3 API for more fine-grained control of the [CORS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/enabling-cors-examples.html) or [S3 canned ACL](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/managing-acls.html) settings.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. - [Create an Object Storage bucket](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-object-storage-bucket.md): Creates an Object Storage bucket in the specified data center ([region](ref:get-regions)). If the bucket already exists on your account, this operation returns a 200 response with that bucket as if the API just created it.  > 📘 > > - Accounts with negative balances can't access this operation. > > - The API still supports the `clusterId` equivalent (`us-west-1`) when setting a `region` for a new bucket, but you should use the `regionId` (`us-west`) instead. > > - This operation has specific [rate limits](ref:rate-limits#specific-operation-rate-limits). > > - You can use an outside API, such as the [Ceph Object Gateway S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/bucketops/#put-bucket) for more options.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `object_storage:read_write`
- [Create a URL for an object](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-object-storage-object-url.md): Creates a pre-signed URL to access a single object in a bucket. Use it to share, create, or delete objects by using the appropriate HTTP method in your request body's `method` parameter.  > 📘 > > You can use an outside API, such as the [Ceph Object Gateway S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/) for more options.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. ACLs define who can access your buckets and objects, and specify the level of access granted to those users.  > 📘 > > You can use an outside API, such as the [Ceph Object Gateway S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/objectops/#set-object-acl) for more options.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `object_storage:read_write`
- [Update access to an Object Storage bucket](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/put-storage-bucket-access.md): Update basic cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) and [S3 canned access control list (ACL)](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/acl-overview.html#canned-acl) settings.  > 📘 > > You can use the S3 API for more fine-grained control of the [CORS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/enabling-cors-examples.html) or [S3 canned ACL](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/managing-acls.html) settings.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. A successful request triggers an `obj_access_key_create` [event](ref:get-events).  > 📘 > > - Accounts with negative balances can't access this operation. > > - This operation has specific [rate limits](ref:rate-limits#specific-operation-rate-limits).  **Unlimited vs. limited access keys**  An unlimited access key grants full access to all of your buckets in each region you name, using the `regions` array. A limited access key lets you name specific buckets where you need to manage content, using the `bucket_access` array.  > 👍 There's a tutorial > > We offer workflows for both an [unlimited access key](ref:create-an-unlimited-access-key) and a [limited access key](ref:create-a-limited-access-key).   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. - [Delete a placement group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-placement-group.md): Deletes a placement group you have permission to `read_write`.  - Deleting a placement group can't be undone.  - All Linodes need to be [removed](ref:post-group-unassign) before you can delete a placement group.  - If your placement group is non-compliant, you can't delete it. You need to wait for our help to bring it [compliant](https://www.linode.com/docs/products/compute/compute-instances/guides/placement-groups/#non-compliance-precedence).  <<LB>>  > 📘 > > To run this operation, your user needs the `add_linodes` [grant](ref:get-user-grants). - [Get a placement group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-placement-group.md): View a specific placement group by ID.  > 📘 > > Your user needs at least `read-only` [permission](ref:get-user-grants) to all Linodes included in a placement group to view it. If you don't have this access and call this operation, the API returns a 403 error. Talk to your local account administrator about managing permissions.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. - [List placement groups](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-placement-groups.md): Returns a paginated list of placement groups you have permission to view.  > 📘 > > Your user needs at least `read-only` [permission](ref:get-user-grants) to all Linodes included in a placement group to view it. Placement groups that contain Linodes that you don't have this permission for are left out of the response. If all placement groups contain Linodes that lack this permission, the API returns a 403 error. Talk to your local account administrator about managing permissions.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. - [Unassign a placement group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-group-unassign.md): Remove a Linode from your placement group.  > 📘 > > To run this operation, your user needs the `add_linodes` [grant](ref:get-user-grants) and `read-write` [permission](ref:get-user-grants) to the Linodes you want to remove from the group. - [Create a placement group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-placement-group.md): Creates a placement group on your account. Placement groups disperse your Linodes across physical machines (hosts) in one of our compute regions. Depending on your workload requirements, you may need your Linodes to follow specific strategies:  - __Grouped-together model__. You may want them placed close together to reduce latency between Linodes that are used for an application or workload. Affinity placement groups follow this model.  - __Spread-apart model__. You may want to disperse them across several hosts to support high availability, for example when required for failover. Anti-affinity placement groups follow this model.  To run this operation, your user needs the `add_linodes` [grant](ref:get-user-grants). Talk to your local account administrator about grant management for your user.  > 👍 There's a tutorial > > We offer an example API [workflow](ref:create-a-placement-group) you can follow to create a placement group and add Linodes to it.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `linodes:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli placement group-create \   --label PG_Miami_failover \   --region us-mia \   --placement_group_type "anti_affinity:local" \   --placement_group_policy strict ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Update a placement group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/put-placement-group.md): Change the `label` for a specific placement group. This is the only value you can update. However, you can [add](ref:post-group-add-linode) more Linodes or [remove](ref:post-group-unassign) existing ones.  > 📘 > > To update a placement group, your [user grants](ref:get-user-grants) need to include `read_write` permission to all of the Linodes in the group. Talk to your local account administrator about grant management for your user.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `linodes:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli placement group-update 528 \   --label PG_Miami_failover_update ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Revoke app access](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-profile-app.md): Expires this app token. - [Get an authorized app](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-profile-app.md): Returns information about a single app you've authorized to access your account.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `view_profile_app` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_only`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli profile app-view 1234 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)

- [Revoke a trusted device](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-trusted-device.md): Revoke an active trusted device for your user. - [List grants](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-profile-grants.md): This returns a GrantsResponse describing what the acting User has been granted access to.  For unrestricted users, this will return a  204 and no body because unrestricted users have access to everything without grants.  This will not return information about entities you do not have access to.  This operation is useful when writing third-party OAuth applications to see what options you should present to the acting User.  For example, if they do not have `global.add_linodes`, you might not display a button to deploy a new Linode.  Any client may run this operation; no OAuth scopes are required.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `list_profile_grants`

- [Revoke a personal access token](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-personal-access-token.md): Revokes a personal access token. The token will be invalidated immediately, and requests using that token will fail with a 401. It is possible to revoke access to the token making the request to revoke a token, but keep in mind that doing so could lose you access to the api and require you to create a new token through some other means.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. - [Create a personal access token](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-personal-access-token.md): Creates a personal access token for your User. The raw token will be returned in the response, but will never be returned again afterward so be sure to take note of it. You may create a token with _at most_ the scopes of your current token. The created token will be able to access your account until the given expiry, or until it's revoked.  __Parent and child accounts__  In the context of the [parent and child accounts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/parent-and-child-accounts-for-akamai-partners) feature, delegate users can't use personal access tokens on child accounts. You need to [create a delegate user token](ref:post-iam-delegation-profile-child-account-token) instead.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `create_profile_pat` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli profile token-create \   --scopes '*' \   --expiry '2018-01-01T13:46:32' \   --label linode-cli ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Update a personal access token](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/put-personal-access-token.md): Updates a Personal Access Token.  __Parent and child accounts__  In the context of the [parent and child accounts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/parent-and-child-accounts-for-akamai-partners) feature, delegate users can't use personal access tokens on child accounts. You need to [create a delegate user token](ref:post-iam-delegation-profile-child-account-token) instead.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. - [Delete a phone number](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-profile-phone-number.md): Delete the verified phone number for the User making this request.  Use this operation to opt out of SMS messages for the requesting User after a phone number has been verified with the [Verify a phone number](ref:post-profile-phone-number-verify) operation.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. - [Verify a phone number](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-profile-phone-number-verify.md): Verify a phone number by confirming the one-time code received via SMS message after running the [Send a phone number verification code](ref:post-profile-phone-number) operation.  - Verification codes are valid for 10 minutes after they are sent.  - Only the same User that made the verification code request can use that code with this operation.  Once completed, the verified phone number is assigned to the User making the request. To change the verified phone number for a User, first run the [Delete a phone number](ref:delete-profile-phone-number) operation, then begin the verification process again with the [Send a phone number verification code](ref:post-profile-phone-number) operation.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `verify_profile_phone_number` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli phone verify \   --otp_code 123456 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Send a phone number verification code](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-profile-phone-number.md): Send a one-time verification code via SMS message to the submitted phone number. Providing your phone number helps ensure you can securely access your Account in case other ways to connect are lost. Your phone number is only used to verify your identity by sending an SMS message. Standard carrier messaging fees may apply.  - By accessing this operation you are opting in to receive SMS messages. You can opt out of SMS messages by running the [Delete a phone number](ref:delete-profile-phone-number) operation after your phone number is verified.  - Verification codes are valid for 10 minutes after they are sent.  - Subsequent requests made prior to code expiration result in sending the same code.  Once a verification code is received, verify your phone number with the [Verify a phone number](ref:post-profile-phone-number-verify) operation.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `send_profile_phone_number_verification_code` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli phone sms-code-send \   --iso-code US \   --phone-number 555-555-5555 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Get user preferences](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-user-preferences.md): View a list of user preferences tied to the OAuth client that generated the token making the request. The user preferences endpoints allow consumers of the API to store arbitrary JSON data, such as a user's font size preference or preferred display name. User preferences are available for each OAuth client registered to your account, and as such an account can have multiple user preferences.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `view_user_preference` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. - [Update a user's preferences](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/put-user-preferences.md): Updates a user's preferences. These preferences are tied to the OAuth client that generated the token making the request. The user preferences endpoints allow consumers of the API to store arbitrary JSON data, such as a user's font size preference or preferred display name. An account may have multiple preferences. Preferences, and the pertaining request body, may contain any arbitrary JSON data that the user would like to store.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. - [Disable two factor authentication](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-tfa-disable.md): Disables two-factor authentication (2FA) for your user. Once successful, login attempts from untrusted computers will only require a password. Keep 2FA enabled to protect your account.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `disable_profile_tfa` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli profile tfa-disable ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Create a two factor secret](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-tfa-enable.md): Generates a secret key for your user. To enable two-factor authentication (2FA), enter this secret into your third-party authenticator application. To complete the 2FA setup, use the [Enable two-factor authentication](ref:post-tfa-confirm) operation to enter a one-time passcode (OTP) from your authenticator app. Once enabled, logins from untrusted computers are required to provide an OTP before they are successful.  Run the [Answer security questions](ref:post-security-questions) operation.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `create_profile_tfa_secret` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli profile tfa-enable ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Get a region's availability](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-region-availability.md): Returns availability data for a single Region.   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli regions view-avail us-east ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)

- [Delete a resource lock](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-resource-lock.md): __Beta__ Delete a specific resource lock. Once deleted, the resource it was assigned to is unlocked, and that resource can be deleted. This operation can only be accessed by account [users](ref:get-users) with _unrestricted_ access. Talk to your local account administrator about access management.  > 📘 > > For specific delete instructions and requirements, see the relevant resource's delete operation documentation.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. - [Create a resource lock](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-resource-lock.md): __Beta__ Create a lock for a resource, to help prevent you from inadvertently deleting it or modifying some aspects of it. You can apply a lock to a single resource, or to a resource and all of its sub-resources. This operation can only be accessed by account [users](ref:get-users) with _unrestricted_ access. Talk to your local account administrator about access management.  Once a resource has a lock, that resource (and its sub-resources, if applicable) can't be deleted, regardless of your user permission. You can unlock a resource by running the [Delete a resource lock](ref:delete-resource-lock) operation for the specific lock.  > 👍 There's a tutorial > > We offer an example API [workflow](ref:create-a-resource-lock-for-a-linode) you can follow to set up a resource lock on a Linode.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli resource-locks create \ --entity_id 8642531 \ --entity_type linode \ --lock_type cannot_delete \ ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Create a support ticket attachment](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-ticket-attachment.md): Adds a file attachment to an open support ticket on your account. Use an attachment to help customer support resolve your ticket. The file attachment is submitted in the request as `multipart/form-data` type. Accepted file extensions include: `.gif`, `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.pjpg`, `.pjpeg`, `.tif`, `.tiff`, `.png`, `.pdf`, or `.txt`.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. - [Open a support ticket](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-ticket.md): Open a support ticket. A ticket can only target a single, specific entity. - [Delete a tag](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-tag.md): Removes a tag from all objects and deletes it. - [Create a tag](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-tag.md): Creates a new tag and lets you optionally add it to specific objects. Tags are labels you can attach to objects in your account. Use them to specify and group attributes of objects that are relevant to you. Currently, you can add a tag to your `linodes`, your `nodebalancers`, the `domains` for your Linodes, and the `volumes` on your Linodes.  > 📘 > > - This operation can only be accessed by account users with _unrestricted_ access. Talk to your local account administrator about access management. > > - If you don't add a tag to a supported object with this operation, you can use that object's update operation to later add the tag you created.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `account:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli tags create \   --label 'example tag' \   --linodes 123 \   --linodes 456 \   --volumes 9082 \   --volumes 10003 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [List volume types](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-volume-types.md): Returns Block Storage volume types and prices, including any region-specific rates.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. - [Delete a volume](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-volume.md): Delete a Block Storage volume. Some conditions apply when deleting a volume:  - Deleting a volume can't be undone.  - Deleting stops billing for the volume. You'll be billed for the time the volume was active during the current billing period.  - You can't delete a volume that's attached to a Linode currently being [migrated](ref:post-migrate-linode-instance). You need to wait for the migration to complete.  - You can't delete a volume that has a `cannot_delete` or `cannot_delete_with_subresources` [resource lock](ref:post-resource-lock) set for it.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `delete_volume` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `volumes:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli volumes delete 12345 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Get a volume](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-volume.md): Get information about a specific Block Storage volume.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. - [Attach a volume](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-attach-volume.md): Attach a Block Storage volume to an existing Linode.  > 📘 > > - The volume and its target Linode need to exist in the same `region`. You can run the [List volumes](ref:get-volumes) and [List Linodes](ref:get-linode-instances) operations and review the `region` for each. > > - There's a maximum number of volumes that can be attached to a single Linode configuration. This number varies, based on the Linode's plan, with higher plan types supporting more volumes. The maximum follows a formula of the Linode plan's `total_ram / 1024`, with a minimum of 8 and a total maximum of 64 volumes. Rescue configurations support a maximum of 8.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `attach_volume` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `volumes:read_write, linodes:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli volumes attach 12345 \   --linode_id 12346 \   --config_id 23456 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Clone a volume](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-clone-volume.md): Target an existing Block Storage volume to create a new one. The resulting volume will be the same size and include the same data. A new volume will incur a charge on your account.  > 📘 > > Only a volume with a `status` of `active` can be cloned. Run the [List volumes](ref:get-volumes) operation to view the `status` of each of your volumes.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `clone_volume` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `volumes:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli volumes clone 12345 \   --label my-volume ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Detach a volume](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-detach-volume.md): Detach a Block Storage volume from a Linode. - [Create a volume](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-volume.md): Creates a [Block Storage](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-block-storage) volume on your account. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `list_vpc_ip_addresses` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `ips:read_only`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs ip-list 123 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [List VPC IP addresses](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-vpcs-ips.md): Returns a paginated list of all VPC IP addresses and address ranges on your account.  > 📘 > > If a Linode has several configuration profiles that include a VPC interface, address information for all of them is listed in the response. Since VPCs can use the same address space, you may see duplicate IP addresses.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `list_vpcs_ip_addresses` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `ips:read_only`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs ips-all-list ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Delete a VPC subnet](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-vpc-subnet.md): Delete a single VPC subnet. A successful request triggers a `subnet_delete` event.  > 📘 > > You need to delete all network interfaces, managed databases and NodeBalancers that this subnet is assigned to before you can delete it. If interfaces are active, the associated Linode needs to be shut down before they can be removed. For more information see: > > - [Delete a Linode interface](ref:delete-linode-interface) and [Delete a configuration profile interface](ref:delete-linode-config-interface) > - [Delete a MySQL Managed Database](ref:delete-databases-mysql-instance) and [Delete a PostgreSQL Managed Database](ref:delete-databases-postgre-sql-instance) > - [Delete a NodeBalancer](ref:delete-node-balancer)   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `delete_vpc_subnet` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `vpc:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs subnet-delete $vpcId $vpcSubnetId ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Get a VPC subnet](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-vpc-subnet.md): Get information about a single VPC Subnet.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `view_vpc_subnet`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs subnet-view $vpcId $vpcSubnetId ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [List VPC subnets](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-vpc-subnets.md): Get information about all VPC Subnets associated with a VPC.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `view_vpc_subnets`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs subnets-list $vpcId ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Create a VPC subnet](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-vpc-subnet.md): Create a VPC Subnet. A successful request triggers a `subnet_create` event.  After you create a VPC subnet, you can attach it to a Linode by assigning the subnet to one of the Linode's network interfaces. To do this, you can run the following operations:  - [Create a Linode](ref:post-linode-instance) - [Create a config profile](ref:post-add-linode-config) - [Update a config profile](ref:put-linode-config) - [Add a configuration profile interface](ref:post-linode-config-interface)  You can also attach managed databases and NodeBalancers to a subnet.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `create_vpc_subnet` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `vpc:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs subnet-create $vpcId \   --label cool-vpc-subnet \   --ipv4 10.0.1.0/24 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Update a VPC subnet](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/put-vpc-subnet.md): Update a VPC Subnet. A successful request triggers a `subnet_update` event.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `update_vpc_subnet` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `vpc:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs subnet-update $vpcId \   --label cool-vpc-subnet ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Delete a VPC](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/delete-vpc.md): Delete a single VPC and all of its Subnets.  - A successful request triggers a `vpc_delete` event and `subnet_delete` events for each deleted VPC Subnet. - All of the VPC's subnets need to be eligible for deletion. You need to delete all network interfaces each subnet is assigned to. If those interfaces are active, you need to shut down the associated Linodes first. If any subnet can't be deleted, it prevents the VPC and its subnets from being deleted. - You can't delete a VPC if a NodeBalancer or a managed database is attached to one of its subnets.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `delete_vpc` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `vpc:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs delete $vpcId ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Get a VPC](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-vpc.md): Get information about a single VPC.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `view_vpc`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs view $vpcId ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [List VPCs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-vpcs.md): Display all VPCs on your account.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `view_vpc`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs list ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Create a VPC](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/post-vpc.md): Create a new VPC and optionally associated VPC Subnets. A successful request triggers a `vpc_create` event and `subnet_create` events for any created VPC Subnets.  After you create a VPC, you can attach it to a Linode by assigning a VPC subnet to one of the Linode's network interfaces. To do this, you can run the following operations:  - [Create a Linode](ref:post-linode-instance) - [Create a config profile](ref:post-add-linode-config) - [Update a config profile](ref:put-linode-config) - [Add a configuration profile interface](ref:post-linode-config-interface)  You can also attach managed databases and NodeBalancers to a subnet.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `create_vpc` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `vpc:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs create \   --description "A description of my VPC." \   --label cool-vpc \   --region us-east \   --subnets.label cool-vpc-subnet \   --subnets.ipv4 10.0.1.0/24 ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)
- [Update a VPC](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/put-vpc.md): Update an existing VPC. A successful request triggers a `vpc_update` event.  To update a VPC's Subnet, run the [Update a VPC subnet](ref:put-vpc-subnet) operation.   **Permissions and scopes**  To call this operation, you need permissions, based on the model you're using:  - **Identity and access permissions**. Your user needs a role with these permissions. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/identity-access-cm-available-roles).   - Permissions: `update_vpc` - **OAuth scopes**. Your user needs these scopes assigned. [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/linode-api/reference/get-started#oauth).      - Scopes:   `vpc:read_write`   **CLI**  ```shell linode-cli vpcs update $vpcId \   --description "A description of my VPC."   --label cool-vpc ```  [Learn more](https://techdocs.akamai.com/cloud-computing/docs/getting-started-with-the-linode-cli)

# Live Archive Management API Documentation

## API Reference
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/api-errors.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/api-get-started.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/api-summary.md)
- [Live Archive Management API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/api.md)
- [Concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/concepts.md)
- [Rate limits](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/rate-limits.md)
- [List archives](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/get-stream-archives.md): Lists all archives, or optionally those created within a specified time range.
- [Remove objects](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/delete-stream-endpoint.md): Delete all objects under this endpoint.
- [List endpoints](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/get-stream-endpoints.md): Lists all the endpoints per stream.
- [Get VOD status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/get-vod-eid.md): **For beta customers only.** _Akamai is no longer accepting new members for this beta program._ Use this to query the status of a VOD asset creation operation, using its export ID (`EID`).
- [Create a VOD asset](https://techdocs.akamai.com/live-archive-management/reference/post-vod.md): **For beta customers only.** _Akamai is no longer accepting new members for this beta program._ When you enable archiving for an MSL live stream, you can retain it for a maximum of 31 days. To retain archived content beyond 31 days, use this operation to create a separate VOD asset that MSL stores in Akamai NetStorage. You need to set up the desired NetStorage storage group using the [Live-to-VOD Export](https://learn.akamai.com/en-us/webhelp/media-services-live/media-services-live-4-user-guide/GUID-039CB50F-CB56-49D8-BBDF-E6675E159023.html) option in Akamai Control Center.

## API Reference
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/api-summary.md)
- [API workflow](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/api-workflow.md)
- [Rotate HTTP digest authentication settings](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/rotate-http-digest-authentication-settings.md)
- [Media Services Live 4 Stream Provisioning API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/api.md)
- [Archive stream members](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/archive-stream-members.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/404.md)
- [405](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/405.md)
- [415](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/415.md)
- [422](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/422.md)
- [429](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/429.md)
- [500](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/500.md)
- [HTTP status codes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/http-status-codes.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/errors.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-started.md)
- [API concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/media-services-live-concepts.md)
- [Rate limiting](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/resource-limiting.md)
- [List CDNs](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-cdns.md): Fetch predefined list of CDN providers. You can configure your MSL origin to include multiple CDN providers.
- [List contracts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-contracts.md): Fetch MSL contracts.
- [List CP codes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-cpcodes.md): Obtains the CP code based on the type. You need a CP code to create a stream.
- [Create a CP code](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/post-cpcodes.md): Creates a new MSL CP code. You need a CP code to create a stream or an origin.
- [Generate a key](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-generate-key.md): Specify either an `AKAMAI` or `THIRD_PARTY` key format.
- [List streams being migrated](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-streams-migrations.md): Fetches the list of streams being migrated, their migration type, status, and migration time.
- [Revert stream migration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/post-revert-streams-migrate.md): Reverts the stream migration and puts stream back on MSL4.
- [Migrate streams to MSL5](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/post-streams-migrate.md): Migrates streams from MSL4 to MSL5. Choose the `SOFT` migration type to test one or more streams and [Revert stream migration](ref:post-revert-streams-migrate), if needed. `HARD` migration type permanently moves the stream to MSL5. For details, see the [Migration guide](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/docs/migration-guide) in the MSL5 documentation.
- [Remove an origin](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/delete-origin.md): Deletes the specified origin.
- [List live origins](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-msl-origin-origins.md): Retrieves a list of all live origins associated with the profile by default, or optionally filtered live origins.
- [List CP codes per origin](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-origin-cpcodes.md): Fetches available CP codes for a new origin.
- [Get an origin](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-origin.md): Retrieves detailed information about the requested origin.
- [Create a new origin](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/post-origin.md): Create a Media Services live origin for the stream. You can associate this origin hostname with an Adaptive Media Delivery configuration.
- [Update an origin](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/put-origin.md): Updates certain fields of the origin. You can also use this API to rotate keys. Populate `previousKey` and `previousKeyExpiryDate` fields if you need your old keys to be valid for a certain amount of time even after rotating.
- [List publishing locations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-publishing-locations.md): Get valid encoder locations.
- [Remove a stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/delete-stream.md): Deletes the specified stream. If you delete an archived stream, but set `purgeContent=false` the stream will be permanently deleted, but any archived content saved on NetStorage will continue to incur billing. If you set `purgeContent=true`, the stream will be permanently deleted and any archived content saved on NetStorage for this stream will also be deleted and cannot be restored.
- [Get a stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-stream.md): Retrieves detailed information about the requested stream.
- [List streams](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-streams.md): Retrieves a list of all streams available to a user. Supports optional URL Parameters for pagination and sorting of the results.
- [Create a stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/post-stream.md): Provisions a new stream. To create an ingest stream using Media Services Live 4, you need to:  - Set up an ingest for the stream - Set up an archive for the stream. For details on the types of archive you can specify, see [Archive stream members](ref:archive-stream-members). - Set up Media Services Live origin for the stream.  The process of creating or updating any stream changes its status to `PENDING`. When all of the steps are complete, the stream status changes to `PROVISIONED`. If any of the steps fail, the stream status changes to `FAILED`. It takes two to three hours to provision a stream.
- [Update a stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/put-stream.md): Modifies an existing stream. The request body object must be identical in structure to the Get Stream Details response object. You can edit the `name`, `ingestAccelerated`, `password`, `dvrWindow`, `allowedIps`, and `additionalEmailIds` members. If you try to edit any other member, the API returns a `400` error. If you want to update the HTTP digest authentication `username`, `password`, or `expiryDate` for a live stream, refer to these examples [Rotate HTTP digest authentication settings](ref:rotate-http-digest-authentication-settings).
- [List VOD origins](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl/reference/get-vod-origin.md): List all of the VOD origins.

You can query data collected at a one minute aggregation with ten percent sampling frequency and a purge window of seven days. The maximum duration is two minutes per query.

## API Reference
- [Trigger an alert](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/post-alert-trigger.md): Triggers a new alert from an external application.
- [Clear an alert](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/post-clear-alert.md): Clears a new alert from an external application.
- [Delete an annotation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/delete-annotation.md): Delete an annotation.
- [Get an annotation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-annotation.md): Get an annotation by ID.
- [List annotations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-annotations.md): Query for a list of annotations.
- [Create an annotation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/post-annotation.md): Create a new annotation from an external application.
- [Update an annotation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/put-annotation.md): Update an annotation by ID.
- [mPulse concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/api-concepts.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/api-summary.md)
- [mPulse Beacons](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/beacons.md)
- [mPulse API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/api.md)
- [mPulse Queries](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/queries.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/404.md)

- [500](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/500.md)

- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-started.md)
- [Rate limiting](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/rate-limiting.md)
- [Get a beacon configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-config.md): You need the API Key for your mPulse app, as well as the additional required fields covered below. This operation gets the data needed in order to run the Send a Beacon operation, such as `h.key`, `h.d`, `h.t`, `h.cr` and `beacon_url`.  You need to run this operation before you can send a beacon.
- [Send a beacon](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/post-beacons.md): Send performance data to mPulse.  To send a beacon, you need to first run the [Get a beacon configuration](ref:get-config) operation. From the response, you can use the `beacon_url` to send a beacon.  See [What is in an mPulse beacon](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse-boomerang/docs/whats-in-an-mpulse-beacon) for the full list of available beacon parameters.
- [Create an mPulse domain](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/put-domain.md): Create a new mPulse domain (app) with the properties specified in the payload.
- [Delete an object](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/delete-objects.md): Delete a repository object.
- [Get an object](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-object.md): Gets a repository object by type and ID.
- [List objects](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-objects-attribute.md): Gets a list of repository objects, filtered by attributes.
- [Update an object](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/post-objects.md): Update a repository object.
- [Create an object](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/put-object.md): Creates an object.
- [Get timer values by A/B test](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-ab-tests.md): Gets the Timer value by A/B Test.
- [Get an application error summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-app-error-summary.md): Gets App Error Messages, Codes, Sources or Types.
- [Get timer values by bandwidth](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-bandwidth.md): Gets the Timer value by Bandwidth.
- [Get timer values by browser](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-browsers.md): Gets the Timer value by Browser.
- [Get a timer by minute](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-by-minute.md): Gets the Timer value by minute.
- [Get dimensions over time](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-dimension-over-time.md): Gets the Top Dimensions over Time.
- [Get dimension values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-dimension-values.md): Gets all Values for a dimension.
- [Get timer or metric values by geography](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-geography.md): Gets Timer or Metric values by geography.
- [Get a histogram for timer](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-histogram.md): Gets a histogram for the specified timer.
- [Get metrics per page load time](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-metric-per-page-load-time.md): Gets the Metric Count per Page Load Time.
- [Get metrics by dimension](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-metrics-by-dimension.md): Gets the values of metrics or timers by dimension.
- [Get timer values by page group](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-page-groups.md): Gets the Timer value by Page Group.
- [Get sessions per page load time](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-sessions-per-page-load-time.md): Gets the number of Sessions per Page Load Time.
- [Get a timer summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-summary.md): Gets the median, margin of error, count, and P95/P98 percentiles for the specified timer.
- [Get timers and metrics](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/get-timers-metrics.md): Gets the values for multiple timers or metrics by minute over time. You can use `metric` and `timer` parameters more than once.
- [Delete a token](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/delete-token.md): Delete a token so it can no longer be used for authentication with the API.
- [Generate a token](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mpulse/reference/put-token.md): Authenticates and generates a token. The security token expires after five hours of inactivity.

## API Reference
- [API concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/api-concepts.md)
- [API performance](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/api-performance.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/api-summary.md)
- [Content Purge API workflow](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/content-purge-api-workflow.md)
- [API workflow](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/api-workflow.md)
- [MSL5 API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/api.md)
- [201](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/201.md): Resource created
- [202](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/202.md): The resource was successfully accepted
- [200](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/204-copy.md): Operation was successful
- [204](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/204.md): Successful deletion
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/400.md): Bad Request
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/401.md): Unauthorized request
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/403.md): Access is forbidden
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/404.md): Resource not found
- [409](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/409.md): There is a conflict in the resource (for instance in an attempt to delete an entity that is still active).
- [412](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/412.md): Precondition failed.
- [500](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/500.md): Internal server error.
- [HTTP status codes](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/http-status-codes.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/errors.md)
- [Get started with MSL5 API](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get-started.md)
- [Delete alert configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/delete-v1-alert-configuration-id.md): Delete an alert configuration.
- [Get alert configuration by ID](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get-v1-alert-configuration-id.md): Get a specific alert configuration by ID.
- [List alert configurations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get-v1-alert-configurations.md): List alert configurations.
- [List triggered alerts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get-v1-alert-triggers.md): List alert given alert configuration_id (optional), start_time (optional), end_time (optional), status (optional).
- [Get alert type by ID](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get-v1-alert-type-id.md): Get specific alert type by ID.
- [List alert types](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get-v1-alert-types.md): List alert types.
- [Activate alert configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/post-v1-alert-configuration-id-activate.md): Activate an alert configuration. Start sending the alert notification email.
- [Deactivate alert configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/post-v1-alert-configuration-id-deactivate.md): Deactivate an alert configuration. Stop sending the alert notification email.
- [Subscribe to alert configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/post-v1-alert-configuration-id-subscribe.md): Subscribe to an alert configuration. Start receiving the alert email notification.
- [Unsubscribe from alert configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/post-v1-alert-configuration-id-unsubscribe.md): Unsubscribe from an alert configuration. Stop receiving the alert email notification.
- [Create alert configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/post-v1-alert-configuration.md): Create a new alert configuration.
- [Update alert configuration](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/put-v1-alert-configuration-id.md): Update an existing alert configuration.
- [Create CP Tag](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/create_cptag.md): Creates a new CP Tag.
- [List CP Tags](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_cptags.md): Lists all accessible CP Tags.
- [Create event](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/create_event.md): Create a new event.
- [Delete event retention buffer](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/delete_event_retention_buffer.md): Delete an event's retention buffer. This will mark recorded content as unavailable within a given time range
- [Delete event](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/delete_event.md): Delete an event.
- [Get event details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get_event.md): Get detailed information about an event.
- [List event's deleted retention buffer](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_event_deleted_retention_buffer.md): Lists all time ranges of segments marked as unavailable for the specified event.
- [List events](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_events.md): List all events for a stream.
- [Activate log destination](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/activate_log_destination.md): Activate a log destination to start exporting logs.
- [Create log destination](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/create_log_destination.md): Create a new log destination for a stream.
- [Deactivate log destination](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/deactivate_log_destination.md): Deactivate a log destination to stop exporting logs.
- [Delete log destination](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/delete_log_destination.md): Delete a log destination.
- [Get log destination details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get_log_destination.md): Get detailed information about a log destination.
- [List log destinations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_log_destinations.md): List all log destinations for a stream.
- [Update log destination](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/update_log_destination.md): Update an existing log destination.
- [Create origin server](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/create_origin.md): Creates a new origin server. Hostname must be unique and zones must be different. Any optional field that is omitted will use its default value as documented on each field.
- [Delete origin](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/delete_origin.md): Permanently removes an origin server. Fails if origin has active streams.
- [Get origin details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get_origin.md): Retrieves origin server configuration and status.
- [List origins](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_origins.md): Lists all accessible origin servers. Admins see all origins in the system.
- [Update origins](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/put_origins.md): Update an existing origin's configuration.
- [List publishing locations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_publishing_locations.md): List all accessible publishing locations.
- [Create ingest credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/create_ingest_credential.md): Create new ingest credentials.
- [Delete ingest credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/delete_ingest_credential.md): Remove ingest credentials.
- [List ingest credentials](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_ingest_credentials.md): List all ingest credentials for a stream.
- [Update ingest credential](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/update_ingest_credential.md): Update ingest credential metadata.
- [Create stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/create_stream.md): Create a new stream with the specified configuration. Any optional field that is omitted will use its default value as documented on each field.
- [Delete stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/delete_stream.md): Delete a stream and its associated resources.
- [Get stream details](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get_stream.md): Get detailed information about a specific stream, including origin hostname details for both primary and backup locations.
- [Import a v4 stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/import_stream.md): Imports an existing stream configuration from v4 format.
- [List streams](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/list_streams.md): List all accessible streams.
- [Update stream](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/update_stream.md): Update an existing stream's configuration. This is a full update; omitted optional fields will be reset to their default values, not preserved. See each field description for details.
- [Retrieve usage data](https://techdocs.akamai.com/msl5-harmonic/reference/get-v1-usage-startdate-startdate-enddate-enddate.md): Retrieve usage data for specified date range and filters. Returns ingest, storage, and processing metrics aggregated by date, contract, and CP tag.

## API Reference
- [API concepts](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/api-concepts.md)
- [Get started](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/api-get-started.md)
- [API Hypermedia](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/api-hypermedia.md)
- [API summary](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/api-summary.md)
- [API workflow](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/api-workflow.md)
- [mTLS Edge Truststore API v2](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/api.md)
- [Enumeration values](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/enumeration-values.md)
- [400](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/400.md)
- [401](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/401.md)
- [403](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/403.md)
- [404](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/404.md)
- [405](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/405.md)
- [406](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/406.md)
- [409](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/409.md)
- [415](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/415.md)
- [422](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/422.md)
- [429](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/429.md)
- [500](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/500.md)
- [503](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/503.md)
- [Errors](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/errors.md)
- [Object versioning](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/object-versioning.md)
- [Rate and resource limiting](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/rate-resource-limit.md)
- [Delete a version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/delete-ca-set-version.md): Marks the CA set version (`versionNumber`) in a specified CA set (`caSetId`) for future deletion without removing it from the system.  When you [Create a version](ref:post-ca-set-version), the new CA set version takes the subsequent `versionNumber` irregardless of earlier versions scheduled for deletion.
- [List certificates](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-ca-set-version-certificates.md): List certificates for the specified version.
- [Get a version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-ca-set-version.md): Get the CA set version details.
- [List versions](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-ca-set-versions.md): Versions of the CA set specified by `caSetId`.
- [Get an activation](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-deployment-request-details.md): Get details of an activation or deactivation request specified by `activationId`.
- [Get version activations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-version-deployment-request-details.md): Get details of an activation or deactivation request by set ID and version number.
- [Activate a version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/post-activate-ca-set-version.md): Activate the certificates for the version on the specified network.
- [Create a version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/post-ca-set-version.md): Create a new CA set version in the CA set specified by `caSetId`. When creating a new version, the API compares the certificates and SHA-1 provided in the request with the existing versions in the CA set. If another version exists with fingerprints for the same certificates, a 422 response provides a link to the version. If the `allowInsecureSha1` option differs between the versions with the same certificates, they're still considered the same CA set version. This prevents creating duplicate CA set versions.
- [Clone a version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/post-clone-ca-set-version.md): Clone a version of the CA set from the CA set specified by `version`. Note that the clone may reflect expired certificates from the original.
- [Deactivate a version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/post-deactivate-ca-set-version.md): Deactivate the certificates for the version on the specified network.
- [Update a version](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/put-ca-set-version.md): Update all the certificates for the version, or the `allowInsecureSha1` that controls what type of certificates to allow.
- [Delete a CA set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/delete-ca-set.md): Delete the CA set for given `caSetId`.
- [List CA set activities](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-ca-set-activities.md): List activities on the CA set, sorted reverse chronologically with recent activities first. Apply `start` and `end` parameters to specify a time range. Specify only `start` or `end` to include all activities after or before a given time.
- [Get CA set associations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-ca-set-associations.md): Get a CA set Associations.
- [Get a CA set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-ca-set.md): Get details of a CA set.
- [List CA sets](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-ca-sets.md): List CA sets created under the account.
- [Get CA set delete status](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-deletion-request-for-ca-set.md): Retrieves the status of a CA set deletion request, including its progress, overall status, and any network-specific failures.
- [List CA set activations](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/get-deployment-request-for-ca-set.md): List all activation and deactivation requests for the CA set.
- [Create a CA set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/post-ca-set.md): Create a CA set with the provided name.
- [Clone a CA set](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/post-clone-ca-set.md): Clones a CA set. If you provide the optional cloneFromVersion parameter, the clone is created from that version; otherwise, it uses the latest version. The cloned set may include expired certificates from the source version.
- [Validate certificates](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-edge-truststore/reference/post-validate-certificates.md): Validates a list of certificates passed in PEM format and returns validation results for each certificate. The operation fails if any certificate is invalid.

- [Patch a client certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-origin-keystore/reference/patch-client-cert.md): Update the client certificate's name or notification emails.
- [Create a client certificate](https://techdocs.akamai.com/mtls-origin-keystore/reference/post-client-cert.md): Create a client certificate with the provided name.
